programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan unexpectedly inherits a winning lottery ticket from a dying man - whose widow is understandably not slow in coming forward to claim the multimillion-dollar jackpot. Ken Kercheval (Dallas) guest stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. Steve launches an investigation into suspicions of corruption among his fellow police officers - who respond by framing him for murder. Dick and Barry Van Dyke star

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. The same gang involved in the Schroeder murder kidnaps Mark's underworld friend Gus Benedict. Jack and Mark try to save him before time runs out and prove Steve's innocence, but find themselves in grave danger. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A major earthquake hits the Los Angeles area, leaving the team working against the clock with limited resources to prevent a broken gas main under Koreatown from exploding

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team tackles a plot to smuggle drugs into the country using drones, while Walter gives Happy and Toby an ultimatum on their new relationship

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In an effort to find the terrorist Parsa, Gibbs and the NCIS team track down one of his cohorts using evidence gleaned from the drone attack which has killed six people and wounded a further 22. Meanwhile, an emotionally shaken McGee struggles with the ramifications of the incident. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, Sean Murray, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The murder of a port authority officer reveals Parsa's possible whereabouts as the team continues the hunt for the terrorist intent on wiping out NCIS. Bishop reveals her past association with the quarry and suggests where she thinks he may be found, but the CIA has intercepted a call that places Parsa in a different location to Ellie's hunch. Mark Harmon and Emily Wickersham star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team uncovers faulty bulletproof vests in an abandoned truck filled with stolen USMC merchandise, and attempts to locate the source of the cargo and any vests that could have been sent to military personnel overseas. Crime drama, guest starring Challen Cates, with Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo confronts a man impersonating a Navy officer, which results in guns being drawn, and the only eyewitness to the case is the Special Agent's father - who is in town to disclose a secret to his son. The 250th episode of the crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A bomb detonates at a military charity concert, killing two men. Called to investigate, Tony and McGee try to determine whether the intended target was the 1980s rocker headlining the act or if the attack was part of a larger scheme against the military. Meanwhile, Jimmy prepares for parenthood. Keith Carradine guest stars, with Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A major earthquake hits the Los Angeles area, leaving the team working against the clock with limited resources to prevent a broken gas main under Koreatown from exploding

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Sam and the team spring into action when fellow agent Dom activates a distress signal. They track down his car, which is riddled with bullet holes, and Eric intercepts a signal from his phone, tracing it to a house that contains nothing but some used medical supplies and a dead body. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. The agents learn the cash has been used to set up a bank robbery intended as a ruse to allow a bank employee to transfer $100million of the terrorists' money that has been frozen by the government in an offshore bank. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. The team's investigations reveal he was carrying classified equipment in relation to his work on a new form of battery that the military had hoped to adopt, and that the explosion - captured on CCTV - is not in accordance with that expected of normal fuel. Meanwhile, Callen latest driving misdemeanour means he must attend traffic school. Chris O'Donnell stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Questioned for beating up schoolboy Paul Herriot, irate father Bryce Lucas admits to Ben and Mickey he did so because he had been sent a videotape of the boy sexually assaulting his daughter - a pupil at the same school. Further investigation uncovers a cycle of abuse involving the disturbed youngster, but his story doesn't quite add up and there are further shocks in store, even for Sun Hill's most worldy wise officers

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Stuart's driver Trevor Jones (Sid Owen) helps CID with useful information about a limousine operator who is rumoured to be supplying cocaine as part of the champagne service. Mickey volunteers to pose as a city banker and gather evidence against the villain, but has a few uncomfortable moments when it becomes clear his cover has been blown

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When Stuart's driver Trevor Jones and a female friend are badly beaten up, the detective sets out to link the attack to a mysterious drug dealer known only as the Postman. Meanwhile, Nate and Ben answer a call to a disturbance at a cafe where a boy has had paint sprayed in his face. The assault has racial overtones and the discovery of narcotics at the scene provides a vital lead for the CID investigation

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor is flattered by the attentions of charming Dr Hunter _ but when his housekeeper dies suddenly, she changes her opinion. Period drama, starring Jemma Redgrave

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: A young Idris Elba puts in an appearance at the Thrift Infirmary, as Lady Cora's pregnant maid is rushed in with labour pains, causing Robert's reluctant return.

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor comes up against the governors of the East London Hospital when they threaten to take over the Thrift. Period drama, starring Jemma Redgrave

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Rag Nymph
programme.desc: Feature-length period melodrama set in Victorian times, charting the relationship between feisty Newcastle rag-woman Aggie Winkowski and 10-year-old orphan Millie Forester. Determined to protect Millie from the seamy world of prostitution, Aggie resolves to take the youngster under her wing. Starring Honeysuckle Weeks, Perdita Weeks, Val McLane, Alec Newman, Crispin Bonham-Carter and Patrick Ryecart

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A Christian Scientist couple refuse medicine for their newborn baby, leaving Sister Julienne questioning the nature of absolute faith. However, the case becomes more complicated when the parents are accused of harming their child. Barbara uses her initiative to communicate with a pregnant Sylheti woman who cannot speak English, although using the patient's young son to translate provides new problems. Sister Mary Cynthia returns to Nonnatus House and a woman catches handyman Fred's eye

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A diabetic teenager faces a tough time after falling pregnant, a situation complicated by her boyfriend's criminal past. Nurse Crane offers what support she can - but the girl's life is endangered when the young lovers go on the run. A case of petty theft brings a group of Irish travellers to the attention of the midwives, who end up treating a newborn infant and looking after an expectant mother, and it seems Fred's romance is on the up when he asks Violet to accompany him to a fundraising dance

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: When a shot is fired at a charity fundraiser, host Malcolm Powell is found dead in his study. The victim's PA is certain she knows who the culprit is - a mystery man named Jack Roberts who had an appointment with her boss and was seen fleeing from the scene of the crime. Richard and his team find the suspect's abandoned car nearby with no clues as to his whereabouts or motive for the killing. However, a background check on the dead man reveals that he and his wife defrauded investors out of millions of pounds back in England. Guest starring Sean Pertwee, Lucy Davis and Julie Graham

programme.title: Zen
programme.desc: The detective is asked to investigate when industrialist Ruggerio Miletti - one of the Ministry's wealthiest backers - is kidnapped. However, when the abductee's lawyer is murdered after an illegal attempt to make a ransom payment, Zen realises that dealing with Miletti's treacherous family could prove to be the most dangerous part of the case. Meanwhile, the new Chief of Police sets about making his presence felt - potentially spelling the end for Zen's budding romance with Tania. Rufus Sewell stars

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh creates havoc for Charlie when he reveals her secret talent. Penny's first day back in Whyhope is headed for disaster until she joins forces with Hugh

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh clashes with Penny's new boyfriend, Meryl plays dirty to win the re-count of the Mayoral election, and Ajax does work experience in the hospital

Meanwhile, a cystic fibrosis sufferer, who is waiting for a heart and lung transplant, collapses in a pub and Lisa witnesses her father's friend go into cardiac arrest. Hywel Bennett, Annette Badland and Nina Wadia guest star

programme.title: The Bill

A con-man makes a fool of Beth and Diane. Sam Kelly guest stars

programme.desc: Ian and Ted are in for a nasty surprise, Carol is alarmed by Frankie's return, Robbie looks for buried treasure, and Sonia has a crush on Sanjay

programme.desc: Dave loses his temper when Donna tells him the truth about their son. Meanwhile, Stubbs struggles with life as an officer. Starring Robson Green

programme.desc: James Herriot visits Yorkshire seeking work as a vet with Siegfried Farnon's practice, but his interview becomes a baptism of fire when goes out on the rounds in the Dales countryside. The first episode of the classic BBC drama, starring Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Part two of two. While in Venice investigating his friend's death, Lovejoy discovers that not only is the beautiful Caterina saving art for herself rather than posterity, but so too is her sister Lavinia. Fulton Mackay, Gwen Humble and Haydn Gwynne guest star, with Ian McShane

programme.desc: It Ain't Half Hot Mum

programme.desc: Mrs Slocombe's flat is occupied by squatters - so young Mr Grace allows her to stay in a showcase apartment in the store. Classic comedy, starring Mollie Sugden and Harold Bennett

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The discovery of an abandoned mattress leads Foggy to embark on an town-wide clean-up campaign. Brian Glover guest stars in the rural comedy, with Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: In his latest attempt to impress Nora Batty, Compo borrows Crusher Milburn's bright red motorcycle and dons racing leathers to become a daredevil biker. Comedy, starring Bill Owen, with Kathy Staff

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: Bacchus and Gently are called in to investigate the death of a miner, which is being treated as suspicious because even though his body was discovered down the pit, records show he was above ground when he disappeared. Alleged victim Arthur Hawkes was a proud union man, and his children confirm he fought long and hard to keep the closure-threatened mine open. But as the detectives dig deeper, they find that not everyone agrees with this portrait of him. Could he really have sold his union comrades down the river? Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby star, with Simon Greenall, Jack Deam and Michael Socha

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Retired detective Steve McAndrew arrives from Glasgow to help reinvestigate one of his original cases when a missing girl's DNA is found at the scene of a petrol station robbery in Clapham, south London. It's clear that the inquiry is a very personal one for McAndrew, and although his personality rubs the team up the wrong way, his methods soon pay dividends. Denis Lawson stars

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Boyd is unsettled when Sarah Cavendish (played by Five Daughters star Eva Birthistle), an officer of equal rank to him, joins the team to investigate the disappearance of a banker who had been struggling to cope with losing his daughter to cancer. Though Cavendish's professional skills quickly prove to be a major asset, her new colleagues discover that a failed operation from earlier in her career is continuing to haunt her, and become concerned for her welfare. Trevor Eve stars, with Sue Johnston and Tara FitzGerald

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh is facing his biggest crisis yet when his love life, career and family are all in desperate need of a help. Also, Charlie plays dirty to win back Matt

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: The staff unite to save one of their own. A former slave turned activist arrives in town, causing a rift between Foster and Mary. programme.desc: Part one of two. The mobile medical team treats patients in the church hall, while Lance and Tess respond to a baby's cry among the debris, but fall into the mineshaft below

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Disgraced detective Zain Nadir is interviewed about drug trafficking in Hayes End Prison, where he is serving 10 years for perverting the course of justice, and agrees to help his colleagues infiltrate the gang involved. Jack is not sure whether he can trust his mole and fears the worst when a riot breaks out just as the police move in to arrest the operation's kingpin, Zain's cellmate Jason Forbes, who makes good his escape in the confusion. TJ Ramini and Lloyd McGuire guest star

programme.desc: Tony, Simon, Bianca, Sarah, Joe, Dan and Mel travel to Blackpool by coach. Dan spikes Sarah's drink, Tiff and Bianca discuss Tiff's pregnancy, and Simon finds Tony in a gay bar

programme.desc: Dan plays a dangerous joke on Sarah with a tab of Ecstasy. Bianca advises Tiffany about Tony. Tony, meanwhile, meets up with Simon who takes him to a gay bar

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer is arrested on suspicion of armed robbery - and to make matters worse, the only way he can clear his name is by admitting he was with a charming ex-girlfriend at the time. programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Havers is sent to Buckinghamshire to investigate the death of a radical left-wing politician's illegitimate daughter, whose body has been found in a river. Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Lesley Vickerage star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The body of a missing computer expert turns up in a hospital morgue under a false name, so the team reopens the investigation into his disappearance. The dead man worked for the Metropolitan Police, giving him access to a great deal of sensitive information - added to which, he was in contact with a group of online hackers. But he died of natural causes - so why was his body hidden, and by who? As the seasoned detectives unravel the mystery, Brian struggles to accept Steve as the new Ucos member, forcing Esther to intervene. Guest starring Sarah Smart and Alex Jennings

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A woman who went missing from a care home in 1986 is found murdered in a burnt-out car, and the team discovers she had recently abducted a teenage girl herself. As the investigation continues, both cases appear to be linked to an urban myth about a character known as the Bag Man - an eyeless villain who kidnaps children. With Eva Birthistle, Tara FitzGerald and Wil Johnson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester City v West Ham United at Etihad Stadium and Leeds United v Aston Villa at Elland Road. The Citizens were looking to reinforce their status as title-favourites against a West Ham side that have performed well this term, while the Villans were hoping to avenge a 3-0 defeat at the hands of Leeds at Villa Park in October. Plus, West Bromwich Albion v Brighton & Hove Albion and Newcastle United v Wolverhampton Wanderers

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr interviews key newsmakers and reviews the weekend's stories. With Rishi Sunak MP, chancellor of the exchequer; Anneliese Dodds MP, shadow chancellor of the exchequer; and Stacey Abrams, US democratic politician and voting rights activist. With news review from Nick Eardley, BBC political correspondent ; and Stephanie Flanders, senior executive editor at Bloomberg and head of Bloomberg Economics

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: The Big Questions

programme.title: Sunday Worship

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Crystal Palace v Fulham (Kick-off 12.00pm). All the action from the Premier League clash at Selhurst Park. Palace have won the last three meetings between the sides, including a 2-1 win at Craven Cottage in October, but Fulham will be determined to end that run of defeats as they are battling to avoid relegation despite some encouraging recent results on their travels

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items reclaimed from Witley Recycling Centre, including a cooker hood and a collection of old jars

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets a couple who have already sold up and are desperate to invest £425,000 in their perfect home in Somerset

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich descend on gardens in desperate need of a makeover - but must stick to the owners' budgets

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in South America, where animals must specialise if they are to carve out a niche. In Patagonia, a puma mother draws on a lifetime's experience to catch prey three times her weight, while rarely seen Andean bears clamber 30 metres into the cloud forest canopy to find elusive fruit. Poison dart frogs use ingenious methods to keep their tadpoles safe, anacondas stalk capuchin monkeys, and swifts make death-defying flights through Igauzu Falls on the border between Argentina and Brazil

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani is taken on a tour of one of London's cemeteries, as she discovers a trove of treasures right on her doorstep and sets out to crack the mystery of a non-native bird. Adam Henson shows how a super-meal is specially concocted for his animals during the winter months, and Tom Heap investigates the controversial decision that allows UK farmers to use a banned pesticide on their fields

programme.title: Attenborough's Life in Colour
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the many ways animals use colour, with cameras built specifically for this series revealing a world normally invisible to human eyes. The peacock's tail has evolved entirely to impress females and is one of the most extreme colour ornaments in the natural world, while mandrill baboons signal their strength and status with striking red and blue face masks, the brighter the colours, the higher ranking the male

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Stonor Park in Oxfordshire, where items assessed include a rare artefact that survived the attack on Pearl Harbour and an LS Lowry painting. There are also ancient carvings from the Indian subcontinent and an intriguing wooden carving with a mysterious past

programme.title: Bloodlands
programme.desc: Protocol will not let Tom Brannick near the island investigation, but driven by his hunger to settle a score from his past, the detective attempts to solve the kidnapping case. With the help of Birdy he is soon in perilous pursuit of a possible connection to the crime, but the question soon arises whether it is Goliath he is hunting, or someone making a false trail. Crime thriller, starring James Nesbitt

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's five Premier League fixtures, including Chelsea v Manchester United at Stamford Bridge and Sheffield United v Liverpool at Bramall Lane. The Blues and United drew 0-0 when the sides met at Old Trafford in October, but both clubs will have been hoping to claim all three points on this occasion as they looked to boost their hopes of at least finishing in the top four. The Blades have struggled so far this season, but will have drawn hope from Liverpool suffering a number of poor results against bottom-six teams this term. Plus, Crystal Palace v Fulham, Leicester City v Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur v Burnley

programme.title: The Rugby Codebreakers
programme.desc: Carolyn Hitt presents a look at the Welsh rugby players during the 100-year amateur era who broke what was considered a taboo by many to switch codes from union to league. Carolyn travels to Huddersfield where in 1895, members of 22 teams decided to resign from the amateur English Rugby Union, with the professional rugby league being born as a result. She also provides an insight into those from South Wales who found great success after crossing codes in the 1920s and the black players who flourished in the 1970s, including Roy Francis and Clive Sullivan

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters examine a one-bedroom flat in Catford, south-east London, a house in Wigton, Cumbria, and an end-of-terrace property close to the illuminations in Blackpool

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate a scam in which a woman ended up £20,000 out of pocket after beginning an online relationship with a US Navy captain

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police try to recover what they suspect are stolen vehicles. Plus, how seized assets are sold off when a ring and a very prestigious Bentley are put up for auction

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Ochuko Ojiri lead two teams competing at Ardingly in West Sussex, while presenter Christina Trevanion looks at the names given to collectors of different antiques

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Nurse Luca ruffles feathers during his first day at the Mill, while Daniel is called to the campus to help two protesters and a woman has an enticing proposition for Valerie

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu are hired to investigate a fire at an award-winning salon, with owner Odette convinced that her old friend and now rival Isobel is behind the blaze and a number of other acts of sabotage. Guest starring Sally Lindsay and Jacqueline Boatswain

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: The return of the property relocation advice programme, with Jonnie Irwin heading to Stratford-upon-Avon in Warwickshire with an adventurous and active couple who have a budget of £650,000

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal returns with the culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by the contestants assisting each of them. In the first edition, Jeremy Pang and Akis Petretzikis are joined by brothers Justin and Adrian

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Carl from Stoke brings in a key that turns out to be not quite what it appears. Alice, from Wales, tries to sell a moulded model advertisement for men's underwear from the early 20th century. Naz from Halifax presents a harmonica that she wants to sell so she and her young son can have a holiday, and Sharon from Hellsby tries to interest the dealers in a Victorian silver belt. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: There are more than a million incidents a year of illegally dumped rubbish in the UK, so why are so few people prosecuted. Reporter Richard Bilton investigates a crime that affects everyone and meets some of those who are fighting back against the fly-tippers

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stacey is feeling remorseful about Ruby's accident and apologises to Martin for everything that has happened. Vinny realises the Panesars need the backing of the Mitchells if their family is to become known in the local area. Isaac builds up the courage to reveal a big secret to Lola, and Gray is furious with Whitney for allowing his children on a playdate with Raymond. How dare she send them to a home where a known killer could show up at any moment?

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack welcome Johnny Vegas, Alex Jones, Martin Lewis and Anna Maxwell-Martin to the comedy panel show on which the teams reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace return with the cookery competition as 40 contestants are put through gruelling culinary challenges to test not just their skills, but also their knowledge of food and ability to cope in a professional kitchen. They have to cook not only for their place in the competition but also for the right to wear a coveted MasterChef apron, with the first five contestants beginning by preparing their signature dish

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The contestants are challenged to create show stopping superhero outfits, with US Drag Race All Star Raven popping in to offer moral support and TV presenter Maya Jama joining Michelle Visage and Graham Norton on the judging panel

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood star Kate Winslet, who talks about her role in romantic Victorian drama Ammonite. Accomplished actor Stanley Tucci discusses his role as a writer suffering dementia in Supernova, Pirates of the Caribbean's Orlando Bloom chats about powerful revenge drama Retaliation, while rising British star Kingsley Ben-Adir talks about his part in sci-fi anthology Soulmates. Plus, Silk City featuring Ellie Goulding perform New Love

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a former commercial property in Stoke bought by owners with a special plan, and terraced houses in Cumbria and Southampton

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate the case of a man in Scotland whose online girlfriend in Ghana has demanded thousands of pounds, but has never visited him

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: This episode follows a Modern Slavery team as they check for signs of workers being exploited. A massive shoe collection goes up for sale when a London drug dealer is sent to jail

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Richard Madley help two teams scour Exeter's Westpoint Arena for items, and Eric Knowles finds out how Exeter City were responsible for starting a footballing dynasty

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Bear's team-building day takes the staff on an exciting scavenger hunt, with some strange encounters and a thrilling race to the finish

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: An energy tycoon hires Frank and Lu to keep an eye on his daughters, one of whom is going off the rails and the other is about to get married - but things quickly get out of hand

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin puts Melton Mowbray and its property market to the test for his buyers, who believe the Leicestershire town is ideal for them to spend £325,000 on their first home

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the culinary duel in which Anna Haugh and Romy Gill help a husband and wife create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients provided by the contestants

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including pieces of pottery saved from a tip, two unusual fire screens and a mirror. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Vinny finds himself in a sticky situation when he is cornered in an alley by a thief he had a run-in with before - and this time the man has brought back-up. Meanwhile, Ben and Kheerat anxiously wait at the Vic for Vinny to arrive. Ruby's revelations lead Martin to boiling point with Kush and Stacey

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Hanssen is thrown when Sahira's seriously ill father is admitted to Keller and he tries to avoid treating him. Cameron is backed into a corner when Bobby arrives on the ward threatening to talk. Ange and Dom clash over his decision to sue the hospital as she is forced to choose between her son and her morals

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Why Is Covid Killing People of Colour?
programme.desc: David Harewood sets out to investigate the high Covid-19 death rates in black and minority ethnic patients in the UK, and what they reveal about health inequality in modern Britain. Visiting health experts and doctors at hospitals in areas with high rates of admissions, he discovers the impact of job opportunities and air pollution in deprived communities can leave the residents far more vulnerable, as well as hearing from patients who feel their concerns were disregarded by health professionals

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman passes out in the classroom after seeing images of his teacher, Miss Watson, tied up, and doctors decide they want to keep him in hospital for observation. Meanwhile, Dylan starts a new job. Starring Freddie Highmore and Vera Farmiga

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman escapes from Deputy Sheriff Zach Shelby's house with the help of his brother Dylan, who creates a diversion - but the girl he found in the basement is left behind. He tells his mother what has happened but she does not believe him, saying that he must sometimes be seeing things that are not there

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets the Bloomers from Mablethorpe in Lincolnshire, who have been competing in Bloom for 10 years but have never won the top prize. With three ambitious new projects planned this year, they hope to not only win their first ever gold award, but also to put their forgotten seaside town back on the map and encourage tourists to visit

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden takes to the skies above Cumbria, visiting Levens Hall where she lends a hand planting spring bulbs, and hears some personal memories of a Lakeland lad. She then travels to Holehird to meet the volunteers who have restored a 19th-century garden to its former glory, before discovering the spur of Beatrix Potter's writing career

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don and the team look back at some of the floral highlights of 2020. It's a celebration of some of the star flowers of gardens, from tulips and roses to salvias and dahlias. There's also another chance to see what viewers were getting up to in their own gardens last year

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Tom Heap explores Northamptonshire's Nene Valley, finding out how a farmer who was fighting flooding is now embracing wetland wildlife to make his land pay. Adam Henson hopes for good news from his ewes as he discovers how many are in lamb, while Charlotte Smith investigates whether Brexit is delivering all it promised for the UK's fishing industry and ecologist Josh Styles reveals the secret world inside a prehistoric plant

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway visits north Wales, where his walk takes him from the village of Abergwyngregyn to the quay at Conwy. This walk also has him heading to the hills, trying his hand at golf and learning how a top secret operation played a major part in winning the Second World War

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Suzanne and Andrew have a huge problem in their lives - a giant derelict swimming pool in their back garden in Plymouth. A succession of landscaping companies have run a mile the moment they gaze into the abyss, and their budget does not even stretch to simply filling it in - so how will designers Tom Massey and Pip Probert transform this unique outdoor space? Meanwhile, Joel Bird comes to the help of an Essex couple with a nightmare patio. Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw and John Cameron help two teams of couples find bargains at an antiques fair in the Royal Highland Centre in Edinburgh, with Anita Manning overseeing proceedings

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Ahead of St David's Day, Sean Fletcher explores beautiful Tintern Abbey in Monmouthshire, and Welsh Rugby legends share their stories of finding faith after hanging up their boots

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Ushaw, County Durham, Catherine Southon examines a toy speedboat and James Lewis discovers a rare poster that came from a morgue. Paul Martin looks at rare Catholic artefacts

programme.title: This Beautiful Fantastic (2016)
programme.desc: A young woman who dreams of being a children's author forges an unlikely friendship with a cantankerous, rich old widower, as well as his housekeeper and an inventor who frequents the library where she works. Drama, starring Jessica Brown Findlay, Tom Wilkinson, Andrew Scott and Jeremy Irvine

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Half of the group head off to the hill stations of the Nilgiri mountains, but the drive involves a treacherous climb that has them on the edge of their seats. Dennis and Paul complete the journey by steam train, passing a station full of monkeys before arriving in Ooty, a town that allows Dennis to fulfil a lifelong dream, as he gets to play a game on the very first snooker table. Sheila, Amanda and Lionel attend a Hindu ancestor ceremony held to remember departed loved ones and reflect on death itself and the fact that they have lost many famous friends over the last year

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: With more than 100 boats in the harbour, Newlyn is Cornwall's biggest fishing port and the outcome of the Brexit trade negotiations could transform the future for its fleet on inshore fishing boats. In the face of an uncertain future, the fleet must prepare for modernisation and whatever challenges or opportunities Brexit may bring

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels the length of North and South America. The expedition begins at the northernmost point in Alaska, journeying south through Canada to the border with the US. In Denali National Park, he witnesses first hand the impact of climate change on a glacier. He also hunts caribou with native Americans, hears from both sides of the conflict over a controversial oil drilling plan, and discovers Vancouver's opioid addiction crisis

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: As the series draws to a close, the farmers begin their preparations for the colder months ahead. Father and Daughter team, Frank and Georgia Hunter continue to find ways to adapt and survive on their 600-acre mixed livestock farm. Meanwhile in Northumberland, Ewan and Emma decide to find a new, bigger farm, and in the Lake District, Matt's vintage temperamental bailer keeps breaking down as he attempts to bring in the hay

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The presenter introduces a look at animals with impressive communication skills, discovering how white-throated sparrows use song to attract mates and the complex vocabulary of prairie dogs. The programme reveals how dolphins are able to address each other by name and chimpanzees use sign language, and also reveals species that communicate using ultrasound, vibrations and parts of the light spectrum beyond the ability of human sense to process

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, joined virtually by a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life, who are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined by special guests Joe Lycett and Judi Love, who will be beamed into the studio to chew over the lighter end of the news spectrum

programme.title: Dylan Moran: Off the Hook
programme.desc: The broadcast premiere of a stand-up performance from the comedian's 2015 UK tour. Dylan Moran playfully scrambles through the struggles of life. He journeys through politics, loneliness and middle age, offering his uniquely kaleidoscopic lens on familiar subjects and distilling pearls of wisdom all along the way

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores Scottish creativity on a journey from Oban to Glasgow. Travelling through the Highlands, Frankie visits the home of Grant Morrison, who has written stories for some of the comic world's most iconic characters. Resuming his trek, he travels from Dunoon to Bannockburn, the site of Robert the Bruce's most famous battle, where he learns how to fight with a sword and shield just like the Scots Knights of the 1300s. Frankie also witnesses young artists brightening up the darker corners of Glasgow and reflects on the city's shameful past, profiting off slavery in the British colonies

programme.title: The Company You Keep (2012)
programme.desc: A respected lawyer is forced to go on the run when a reporter exposes the truth about him - he is really a member of a radical militant group wanted for questioning in a murder case, who has been living under a fake identity for 30 years. As the fugitive goes in search of his old allies for sanctuary, the reporter delves into his past and uncovers disturbing secrets. Thriller based on the novel by Neil Gordon, directed by and starring Robert Redford, with Shia LaBeouf, Julie Christie and Susan Sarandon

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: The fight to stop Sylar has consequences that will change everything as Nathan tries to keep him from meeting the US president. Meanwhile, Hiro learns there are repercussions for regaining his ability, and Matt is forced to go to extraordinary lengths to protect his future with his family. Michael Dorn guest stars, with Zachary Quinto, Adrian Pasdar, Masi Oka and Greg Grunberg

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom's plans are frustrated when Ange blocks his discharge, and he may suffer extreme physical side effects when he takes matters into his own hands. Alex's blissful romance with Donna is rocked when a patient seems to know all about him. Nicky is forced to turn to Louis and Kian when her health issues return, but Cameron couldn't be less interested

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers group is glad when Rocket Dog has her second litter of pups to replenish their dwindling numbers

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Disaster strikes at the Whiskers' burrow, and the Commandos are on the warpath when Zorro disappears

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: As the series draws to a close, the farmers begin their preparations for the colder months ahead. Father and Daughter team, Frank and Georgia Hunter continue to find ways to adapt and survive on their 600-acre mixed livestock farm. Meanwhile in Northumberland, Ewan and Emma decide to find a new, bigger farm, and in the Lake District, Matt's vintage temperamental bailer keeps breaking down as he attempts to bring in the hay

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright demonstrates exercises designed to get muscles moving

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Motivational Monday, with Cel Spellman

programme.title: Bang Goes the Theory
programme.desc: The team investigates whether Britain could ever run out of electricity, with Maggie Philbin spending a day in the control room of the National Grid, whose job is to ensure electricity is always available. Liz Bonnin reports on renewable energy with a visit to one of the UK's largest offshore wind farms and Jem Stansfield shows how and why electricity now relies on gas and reveals a potential fuel of the future - liquid air

programme.title: The Nine to Five with Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey takes five teenagers to different industries for the ultimate work experience, kicking off their journey in a Gordon Ramsay restaurant in London

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from TV chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Eric Knowles takes on Chuko Ojiri at an antiques fair in Somerset, with the two taking very different approaches to spending their £750 budgets

programme.title: The Great Rift: Africa's Wild Heart
programme.desc: How geological forces created the Great Rift Valley in East Africa, and why a string of volcanos running from Ethiopia to Tanzania provides the ideal habitat for some of the world's most extraordinary wildlife. The programme also discovers many newly discovered species have made their homes among the dangerous peaks, where they have developed unorthodox methods of coping with the challenging terrain

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Charles Hanson are in Margam Country Park near Port Talbot, where items include a tea caddy and a book signed by David Livingstone. Paul Martin visits Cardiff Castle to see furniture by William Burges

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nihal Arthanayake, Jake Humphrey, Patsy Kensit and Mae Martin go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team help people with a little land or a beautiful garden generate extra income by creating their own unique B&Bs. They begin by heading to the picturesque Wye Valley to help George and Al transform their battered old family camper van

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: The food writer and former Great British Bake Off judge celebrates the everyday food and ingredients she has always loved, from hearty and wholesome ideas to dishes that are a little bit more indulgent. Mary begins by travelling to Scotland to enjoy the best of the country's staple foods, preparing recipes inspired by her own Scots heritage. She discovers wonderful shellfish in Ullapool, joins chef Tom Kitchin to share the joy of venison cooked on a barbecue and whips up her special whisky cream on the banks of a loch

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of the life of Ernest Rutherford, the Hitchcock films of Cary Grant, the motorways of England, and the Poldark novels, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell asks the questions as the Severns take on the Apres Skiers in the last of the quarter-final matches, with a place in the semi-finals at stake. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between groups of four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues asking what connects naledi in South Africa, Denisova in Siberia, luzonensis in the Philippines and floresiensis in Indonesia

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The quarter-finals continue, with two teams of four students battling it out to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Eddie Izzard: Force Majeure
programme.desc: A stand-up performance from the comedian, offering a glimpse into the mind of the surrealist. The show was recorded during Izzard's mammoth 2013-14 tour, which took in venues in 27 countries across the world

programme.title: Some People with Jokes
programme.desc: The nation's sharpest scientific minds and keenest brains tell their favourite gags, including the conundrum of why the quantum physicist crossed the road

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Omnibus
programme.desc: Celebration of comedy writers Jimmy Perry and David Croft, creators of Dad's Army, It Ain't Half Hot Mum and Hi-de-Hi!. Featuring contributions from cast members and famous fans

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Tom Heap explores Northamptonshire's Nene Valley, finding out how a farmer who was fighting flooding is now embracing wetland wildlife to make his land pay. Adam Henson hopes for good news from his ewes as he discovers how many are in lamb, while Charlotte Smith investigates whether Brexit is delivering all it promised for the UK's fishing industry and ecologist Josh Styles reveals the secret world inside a prehistoric plant

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Bodnant Garden, a National Trust property near Tal-y-Cafn in Conwy, where items assessed include an ecclesiastical candlestick from a very special cathedral and some novelty glass bottles with a hefty price tag. There is also a tandem bicycle and sidecar and a selection of 20th-century automata

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The presenter discusses the challenge of preserving unspoilt wildernesses, from the icy expanses of Siberia to the tropical forests of central and South America. Simon reflects on how his past series have dealt with the causes and impact of climate change, as well as remembering a memorable report exposing the extent of plastic pollution in the ocean. He also recalls meeting indigenous people around the world and hearing their first-hand experiences of ecological damage

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate a scam in which a woman ended up £20,000 out of pocket after beginning an online relationship with a US Navy captain

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams find potentially profitable items at the East of England showground in Peterborough, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp attempts to get to the bottom of an ancient mystery

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Life in Cadgwith Cove, which is Cornwall's last traditional full-time fishing cove. For generations, sons have followed fathers off the beach and out to sea, and now the next generation is set to join the fleet. But while there might be new blood, the old working fabric of the cove itself is now under threat

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: For today's exercise, Mark Wright is joined by Paralympic javelin star Hollie Arnold

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Study tips, with Cel Spellman

programme.title: Bang Goes the Theory
programme.desc: The team explores the technological revolution arising from the world of so-called 'big data' - computers' ability to store and analyse unimaginable amounts of information. Liz Bonnin reveals how the technology behind the computerised monitoring of Rolls-Royce jet engines could be used to detect brain injuries in critically ill patients, and Jem Stansfield builds his own low-tech storage system, using hacksaw blades and magnets. Maggie Philbin explores the darker side of data collection, asking how much personal information is out there - and how easy it is to find

programme.title: The Nine to Five with Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley takes five teenagers to their final work experience placement - Heathrow, Europe's busiest airport

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Ben and Marina Fogle present this month's Lifeline appeal on behalf of the charity Tommy's, who fund pioneering research into miscarriage, stillbirth and premature birth, as well as provide pregnancy health information to parents.

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter S, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Eric Knowles goes head to head with Ochuko Ojiri in Villeneuve-lès-Avignon, France, with Ochuko on the hunt for retro chic, while Eric sniffs out classic French antiques

programme.title: The Great Rift: Africa's Wild Heart
programme.desc: How the diverse array of waterways in East Africa's Great Rift Valley - including rivers, coral seas, caustic springs and waterfalls - helps to sustain the region's wildlife. A wide variety of birds, animals and fish rely on the water for survival, and many have evolved to adapt to the challenges posed by the area's geography

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Will Axon and Mark Stacey examine a telescope that once belonged to a naval captain, and a rare Watcombe clock, as they appraise items brought Powderham Castle in Devon

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Nihal Arthanayake, Jake Humphrey, Patsy Kensit and Mae Martin go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team are in Herefordshire to meet new parents Cheryl and Bill and help get their chalet on a hill from flat-pack to Scandinavian chic

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: Vets at Edinburgh Zoo deal with a zebra with arthritis, while a binturong is being prepared to leave for the south of France as part of a breeding programme, and keepers Vickie and Laura try to weigh a polar bear

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The designers work alone for the first time in the contest, each being assigned to transform a beach hut at Walton-on-the-Naze in Essex, in just two days on a budget of £1000. Working in a small space presents a serious challenge, and while some designers decide to incorporate existing features in their vision, others ruthlessly strip the huts bare in a bid to make their mark. Helping Michelle Ogundehin judge the results is journalist and interior designer Sophie Robinson

programme.title: DNA Family Secrets
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley helps people answer life-changing questions by examining their DNA. In the first episode, she comes to the aid of Richard, who wants to learn if a man claiming to be his biological father is the genuine article, and Charlie, who wants to know what her chances are of contracting the same terminal illness as her father

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, joined virtually by a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life, who are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined by special guests Joe Lycett and Judi Love, who will be beamed into the studio to chew over the lighter end of the news spectrum

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The presenter introduces a look at animals with impressive communication skills, discovering how white-throated sparrows use song to attract mates and the complex vocabulary of prairie dogs. The programme reveals how dolphins are able to address each other by name and chimpanzees use sign language, and also reveals species that communicate using ultrasound, vibrations and parts of the light spectrum beyond the ability of human sense to process

programme.title: Lily: A Transgender Story
programme.desc: Filmed over five years, cameras follow 20-year-old Lily Jones, as she transitions from male to female. During this period she leaves her seaside home in Aberystwyth for a new life in Birmingham, undergoes gender reassignment surgery and finds love

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Pam and Marcus have a big garden surrounded by large established trees, which stretches away from their suburban home in St Albans, Hertfordshire. Now their children have grown up, they want to transform the garden into a space they they can enjoy themselves. Designer Helen Elks-Smith is set on a design that begins with the home, while rival Tom Massey wants to transport them to another world. Which idea will the couple choose? Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Monica Galetti and Giles Coren visit the Yorkshire Dales to work at Swinton Estate, the ancestral home of Lord and Lady Masham. By taking a look the heart of the grand house and its countryside charm, as well as the wider estate, which has opened up to for guests to enjoy, Monica and Giles learn about the challenges of making history pay in the modern world

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Many Faces of
programme.desc: The story of a quiet, dedicated man who might have been a bank manager, but went on to become one of the nation's most popular comedy stars. Ben Elton, Michael Grade, David Renwick and Josephine Tewson are among friends and colleagues who remember his partnership with Ronnie Corbett in sketch show The Two Ronnies, as skinflint grocer Arkwright in Open All Hours and crafty prisoner Fletch in Porridge. There is also a look at his prolific writing career and his work as an actor in serious roles

programme.title: Britain's Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.desc: Andy Torbet dives below the waves in search of the inspiration for the first stone circle, while Chris Packham and Neil Oliver spend the night on an abandoned island as they hunt for clues as to why cultures change. Meanwhile, Shini Somara tests the technology behind a bronze age sauna and the archaeologists uncover a remarkable find

programme.title: Ireland to Sydney by Any Means
programme.desc: Adventurer Charley Boorman and his team look for alternative transport after finding themselves on a sinking cargo boat while en route to Borneo to help with a Unicef vaccination project. After flying to Bali, they embark on a series of journeys involving speedboats, overcrowded ferries and a hand-made boat to reach the shores of Australia. Once there, bad weather thwarts their attempt to take the most direct path to their final destination - Sydney

programme.title: Sorry Angel (2018)
programme.desc: Jacques, a successful writer and single father from Paris, begins an intense romance with Arthur, a young aspiring filmmaker. However, they soon face rejection and sickness. Drama, starring Vincent Lacoste, Pierre Deladonchamps and Denis Podalydès. In French with English subtitles

programme.title: Bute: The Scot Who Spent a Welsh Fortune
programme.desc: Suzanne Packer narrates the story of Victorian aristocrat John Patrick Crichton-Stuart, the third Marquess of Bute, who financed the construction of distinctive Welsh landmarks including the gothic Cardiff Castle, and Castell Coch. One of the richest men in his era, Lord Bute had a passion for architecture, but is also reported to have been a forward-thinking intellectual who supported women's right and striking miners, and Suzanne explores how Wales became the beneficiary of this visionary Scottish lord's extravagant tastes

programme.title: The Many Faces of
programme.desc: The story of a quiet, dedicated man who might have been a bank manager, but went on to become one of the nation's most popular comedy stars. Ben Elton, Michael Grade, David Renwick and Josephine Tewson are among friends and colleagues who remember his partnership with Ronnie Corbett in sketch show The Two Ronnies, as skinflint grocer Arkwright in Open All Hours and crafty prisoner Fletch in Porridge. programme.title: Ireland to Sydney by Any Means
programme.desc: Adventurer Charley Boorman and his team look for alternative transport after finding themselves on a sinking cargo boat while en route to Borneo to help with a Unicef vaccination project. After flying to Bali, they embark on a series of journeys involving speedboats, overcrowded ferries and a hand-made boat to reach the shores of Australia. Once there, bad weather thwarts their attempt to take the most direct path to their final destination - Sydney

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: Cerys Matthews explores the Mabinogion, a medieval book of ancient folk tales which includes the first-ever appearance of King Arthur. Translated from Welsh to English in the 19th century by Charlotte Guest, the influence of the book can be seen today in the works of JRR Tolkien and in Game of Thrones. The presenter examines the original manuscripts, and visits some of the locations in Wales that inspired many of the stories

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint an expansive barn, half covered in a snowy setting of frostbitten foliage and sun-kissed sky

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Tessa Dunlop and Neil Oliver present an insider guide to southern Wales, from the Severn Bridge to St Davids, as they unearth the coast's wild appeal. Tessa builds on 10 years of Coast stories from the area as she seeks new tales and extreme experiences for the guide, including coastal rowing, exploring why Gower was made Britain's first area of outstanding natural beauty, kayaking at Worm's head, and cockle picking at Penclawdd

programme.title: The Story of Welsh Art
programme.desc: Huw Stephens explores Wales' cultural history, beginning on Anglesey where he steps inside a prehistoric burial chamber to witness Welsh art in its earliest form. He also views an exquisite example of Bronze Age artistry in the shape of a gold cape, found by chance in a field in north Wales and now one of the prize exhibits in the British Museum

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Two journalists pick up the trail of $12.5 billion in cash that was allegedly flown out of Libya in the dead of night just months before Colonel Muammar Gaddafi's death. In South Africa, they discover an eyewitness who seems to know all about the money, but before he can provide them with proof the story takes a sinister twist, the first of many

programme.title: St David's Day at the BBC
programme.desc: A rummage through the BBC's music archives, featuring a selection of tracks from some of the most important and innovative Welsh artists of the past few decades. The programme includes performances by Manic Street Preachers, Catatonia, Super Furry Animals, Marina and the Diamonds, Stereophonics, Feeder, Shakin' Stevens, Bonnie Tyler, Shirley Bassey and Tom Jones

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint an expansive barn, half covered in a snowy setting of frostbitten foliage and sun-kissed sky

He also views an exquisite example of Bronze Age artistry in the shape of a gold cape, found by chance in a field in north Wales and now one of the prize exhibits in the British Museum

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: Anatomist Alice Roberts explores Mary Shelley's Frankenstein, questioning the main themes of the novel and asking who could have influenced the author's vision of the story. Plus, the presenter travels to the Villa Diodati in Geneva, where Shelley first conceived the idea of Victor Frankenstein's creature, before examining the original manuscript at the Bodleian Library in Oxford

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a larger-than-landscape featuring layers and layers of mountains as far as the eye can see

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: The visiting head of an African nation turns out to be an old college pal of Hacker - who promptly sets about trying to offload some British-made oil-rigging gear. Satirical comedy set in the world of Westminster, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: The Fall and Rise of Reginald Perrin
programme.desc: Reggie takes his family to a safari park and finds the atmosphere in the car so oppressive that he steps outside to take his chances with the lions. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: Hattie
programme.desc: Ruth Jones stars as Carry On actress Hattie Jacques in this dramatisation of her secret affair with a younger man in the early 1960s, while still married to actor John Le Mesurier. A love triangle develops between the three and Hattie uses her charm and ingenuity to control escalating family tensions and threats of public exposure - but must ultimately make a decision that puts her at risk of being left on her own. Also starring Robert Bathurst, Aidan Turner and Jeany Spark

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.desc: The journalist interviews the writing duo famous for TV comedy series including The Likely Lads, Porridge, Lovejoy and Auf Wiedersehen, Pet, with their most recent collaboration resulting in the two-part drama Spies of Warsaw. The pair are credited as being among the first to introduce regional dialects and working-class sitcoms to television, and discuss their reputation for rescuing foundering Hollywood scripts

programme.title: Britain and the Sea
programme.desc: David Dimbleby investigates the importance of the sea to trade and to the individual livelihoods of coastal communities. He sails along the west coast of Scotland, starting at Craobh Haven and along the Crinan Canal, a waterway designed by East Lothian engineer John Rennie to take 120 miles off the journey from the west coast to Glasgow and safely avoid the turbulent waters around the Mull of Kintyre. He also climbs aboard the last working sea-going Clyde puffer, where he earns his keep shovelling coal in the steam room, and reflects on the role played by the river in both trade and shipbuilding

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Twelve years after the death of his grandson, Risto Kurtti receives a mysterious letter at his home in Helsinki. Finnish psychological thriller, starring Antti Virmavirta

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Convinced that Leo is Elias Leppo, Risto panics when he learns that his grandson Kalle has gone climbing with him. Unsure about Risto's theory, Eeva calls Seppo to try to find out if Elias is still in Finland. In Finnish

She has found a spooky cave rumoured to be haunted by a ghost searching for his own heart, which was stolen several hundred years ago. Mimi also introduces sisters Angel, Melody and Nevaeh, who are super-proud of their traveller heritage and go trick or treating on their plot of land. Another Halloween fan is TikTok phenomenon Fats Timbo, who is Mimi’s Q&A guest and tells us how she has managed to use her platform to empower herself and others. programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework

Trying to win Lauren’s shiny gold stars are team captains Myles and Kathryn, They are joined by Blue Peter’s Lyndsey Russell and comedians Vikki Stone, Ria Lina and Nathan Caton as they all battle it out in a series of hilarious rounds guaranteed to make you laugh. Rounds include Kids Have Arted, Mimecraft, and everyone’s favourite, Pie the Supply! This week they are all pretending to be art teachers, but only one of them is telling the truth. So watch someone get pied in the face before the final round, High School Dropout, where someone from the teams gets binned from a great height!

programme.desc: Celebrity chef and environmental campaigner Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall delivers a special science lesson about sustainability and recycling. Passionate about highlighting the impact humans have on the planet, Hugh focusses on single-use plastics and why they are so unsustainable. He offers advice on how to reduce, reuse and recycle more effectively and how we can all make small changes to live more sustainably.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson stars in an episode focusing on the life of the English monarch, including his struggles with his boring dad Henry VII, before becoming king himself, and going through wives like most people go through toothbrushes. While chatting to Ricky, Ade shares amazing stories about his childhood, falling in love with basketball, winning medals as a Paralympian and his passion for travelling the world.

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. Mr Hammond is on a wildlife trip in New Zealand, and Andy is eager to hear what animals he has spotted. Jen and Andy call Mr Hammond from the Aqua Base, who answers their call standing in a river, wearing full fishing gear. He informs Jen and Andy that he has seen killer whales and is fishing for trout. Suddenly, there is a barking noise and Mr Hammond looks in disbelief as he sees a fur seal pup disappear into the forest. Andy reassures a distressed Mr Hammond that he and Jen will investigate why the seal pup is making its way into the forest. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Donning his adventure gear, Andy takes to the skies in his safari-mobile, jetting off to the south island of New Zealand.

Maddie explains the different layers of our atmosphere and how astronauts travel through it into space and the special suits they wear to protect them. She tries on a real spacesuit and learns about the types of spacesuits they wear to do different jobs. Maddie discovers astronauts need to be fit, healthy and have good agility. programme.title: My First
programme.desc: We follow Mollie and her family as they celebrate and learn more about St David's Day. First, Mollie and her family get busy planting daffodils and leeks. Then it's time to head across to the impressive St David's Cathedral, where they can learn more about who Saint David actually was. At school, Mollie gets the chance to learn about traditional Welsh dress while practicing her Welsh phrases. Some very special school visitors arrive to bring on fun games and songs to sing. After a short stop off at Nana's house, where they make delicious Welsh cakes together, Mollie and her family head over to a local workshop. They get the chance to design their very own lovespoon and watch it actually being made! Before the day is done, it's time to catch the St David's Day festival in Swansea. Mollie gets a chance to take part in lots of fun activities like making dragon wings, spray-painting and trying delicious Welsh food. What an exciting time!

Ferne visits a wildflower garden to find different types of bees, while Rory and his son Sandy visit the park looking for bumblebees. They then return home to build their own bumblebee house. Plus, we find out how bumblebees help to make a third of all the food we eat by a process called pollination.

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. It’s Safari Worlds annual recycling event, and Andy has been busy tidying up and encouraging the guests to recycle their litter. Mr Hammond calls as he is worried that the new clownfish are not settling in okay after their long trip from Borneo. They are leaving the safety of their sea anemone home, which they hardly ever do in the wild. Mr Hammond is at a loss as to why they are doing this, but Andy reassures him that they will get to the bottom of it. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Uploading his wet suit, aqua pack and gizmo, Andy runs to the Aqua Dome’s loading dock and boards his safari-sub, a hi-tech underwater submersible capable of travelling to any location on the planet in the blink of an eye. programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Arthur Sleep is joined by team captains Gail Force and Keith Fitt in this slapstick quiz show featuring guests and clips from Gigglebiz. Joining Gail and Keith for this GiggleQuiz are Wizard Tripwick and Captain Adorable. Round 1 is a picture round featuring hidden objects in photos of DIY Dan and Professor Muddles. Gail’s team turn to the Crystal Ball for help. In Nana’s Panto Showdown, Nana Knickerbocker and her cows are busy making pizzas. After the Crystal Ball refuses to help, Gail’s team are still yet to score a gigglepoint at the halfway point in the show. Round 3 features a clip starring King Flannel trying to get a biscuit. After some more refusal from the Crystal Ball, Wizard Tripwick and Gail struggle to answer the questions themselves. Keith produces his own crystal football to help his team with the answers. After saying goodbye to the Crystal Ball and totting up the final scores, the winners of this GiggleQuiz are revealed.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join four-month-old Annie-Rose and Theo as they discover and explore the world around them through movement. Both children start at home playing with their families. Theo tries pushing and rolling a ball back and forth with his brother and sisters, before playing on his activity mat where he can grab and shake his rattle and kick his legs. Meanwhile, Annie-Rose has a go at grabbing colourful balls and reaching for her soft, cuddly bunny. Mum and dad play a game with her with balloons, as she learns to help them move. Later, Theo goes out with mum to a baby yoga class where he stretches his arms and legs to sing-along songs. Annie-Rose attends a swimming class - singing, swishing and splashing in the pool! When both children get back home, Theo’s mum blows big bubbles in the garden to see how many he can pop, whilst Annie-Rose finishes off on a video call with grandma where she can practice her waving.

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. Mr Hammond has just got back from a canoeing trip in Alaska, and Andy is waiting for the mail to arrive. Over the radio, Jen informs Andy that the mail sack is in the Jungle Den. Mr Hammond found a bald eagle feather while in Alaska but had it sent back, so he is also waiting for the mail. Andy finds the mail sack and starts to go through it. Andy makes a terrible discovery as the seaweed he ordered for the aquatic plants collection has leaked all over the mail - including Mr Hammonds bald eagle feather. Everything is ruined! Palace have won the last three meetings between the sides, including a 2-1 win at Craven Cottage in October, but Fulham will be determined to end that run of defeats as they are battling to avoid relegation despite some encouraging recent results on their travels

programme.title: Money for Nothing
