programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Businessman Adam and his father Ian, who live in a four-bedroom mansion in Essex, swap with single mum Kiptieu, who lives in a small council flat in Abbey Wood, London. With a number of successful businesses under his belt, Adam now travels round the world, interviewing the great and the good from showbusiness, sport and business, including the likes of John Travolta, 50 Cent and Floyd Mayweather, sharing their stories of success with clients who are willing to pay Adam thousands for the inside track. Kiptieu fled war-torn Sierra Leone and is a widow bringing up her three daughters in a two-bedroom ground-floor flat. Kiptieu also recently lost her job because of the Covid-19 pandemic, so the family budget is tighter than ever

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Documentary following British people living it up on the Costa del Sol. Bambi shows newcomers Howard and Lynne around the caravan park and treats them to a Bollywood evening. But unaware to the couple the host has recently gone vegan, so it turns out to be a meat-free knees-up. Kim and Matt reopen their chip shop after a devastating fire which forced them to shut for a year and Harrison is down on the marina, trying to sell tickets for the Black Pearl pirate ship tour

programme.title: About Time (2013)
programme.desc: A single man gets the chance to turn his unhappy life around when his father reveals that they both have the ability to travel through time and change their personal history. He uses his newfound talent to try to improve his love life and win the heart of an American woman he has fallen for. Romantic fantasy comedy, with Domhnall Gleeson, Rachel McAdams and Bill Nighy

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: Part one of two. The singer takes on Australia and New Zealand, beginning her adventures in Sydney, where she experiences the highlights on an open top bus tour. Next stop is a backstage pass to the world-famous Sydney Opera house. Next stop is Tasmania, where she visits a local wildlife sanctuary and its volunteers, comes face to face with its furry residents, and has a run-in with a cheeky wombat. Her first step onto New Zealand soil is at Dunedin, which is rich in Scottish heritage. Later, she heads on to Christchurch, a city devastated by earthquakes

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The programme follows officers from Derbyshire's road crime unit as they target dealers bringing drugs into the county, with PC Nick Lovatt giving chase after a sighting of a suspected dealer in Derby. At police HQ, the cops receive intelligence about a van travelling towards Derbyshire suspected of carrying a large amount of cash. The Road Crime Unit heads into position to intercept the van and surround it on the motorway

programme.desc: The Radford children return to education after months off due to the pandemic, and with 11-year-old Max starting at a new high school his parents anxiously wait to see how his first day went. Noel adopts a radical approach to device addiction and takes the older kids on an adventure, while Sue gets some much-needed quiet time at home with the little ones

programme.desc: This time, paediatric sister Jemma Wray treats two critically ill children - 10-year-old Kai has been having seizures, and 18-month-old Joel is struggling to breathe. Elsewhere, nurse Will Gibbons looks after a 28-year-old patient who has been in a motorbike accident, and 51-year-old Andrew comes into the hospital after damaging his fingers with a circular saw

programme.title: Critical Condition
programme.desc: A mother of one suffers a ruptured brain aneurysm, leaving Dr Nayak just minutes to stop the bleeding. In resus, a patient's heart stops multiple times while Dr Mandava battles to save his life. A 17-year-old girl is critically injured in a car crash. As her parents race to her side, trauma leader Dr Hall works to identify her injuries and get her to emergency surgery

programme.title: Build a New Life in the Country
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke offers advice to a married couple relocating to Ireland, where they plan to live a mortgage-free, self-sufficient existence with their daughter. With a budget of £14,000 to renovate a derelict house that has been empty for more than 30 years, they expect to move into their new home within four months. If they miss their deadline, they face spending Christmas in a caravan

programme.desc: Lisa Holloway meets landlady Audrey and daughter Rhiannon, who have been allowing their guests to take advantage. Located in Leyton just five minutes from east London's Olympic Park, Lisa's chances of turning the homespun venture into a profitable business look promising

programme.title: Killer and Kidnapper: My Father (2018)
programme.desc: A maths tutor finds a way to reconnect with her abducted daughter, but the girl's father will do anything, even kill, to keep the truth hidden. Thriller, starring Jessica Barth and Dylan Bruno

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: Jane sails around South America in style aboard an ultra-luxury ship. Starting in Valparaíso, she embraces her inner Banksy on a street art tour, before beginning her journey south along the Chilean coast. A few days later she reaches the breathtaking glacier on Laguna San Rafael. Here, Jane partakes in the customary and traditional drinking of whisky on ice directly from the glacier. She then ventures further south into deepest Patagonia, stopping off in Punta Arenas. Back on board the ship, Jane enjoys a tour of the bridge with the captain as they make their way to the Falklands

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Levi reports Kyle to the council, and there are arguments all around for the Cannings. Shane's attempts to impress Amy do not work out too well, and only fuel her insecurities

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: It transpires that Justin and Leah cannot see eye to eye on marriage, so they try to find a solution, while Christian loses a patient, which affects his mood terribly

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: The medics explore the theory that a 'broken heart' can cause patients to suffer very real mental and physical problems. Dr Wyne chats to Adolphus, who is struggling to come to terms with the recent death of his wife and best friend within a few weeks of each other. Dr Qamar pays a home visit to Elsie, a sprightly octogenarian whose health has deteriorated since the death of her husband, while Dr Razak treats young Emily, who has inherited a rare genetic condition from her late father which weakens the muscles of the heart

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: A professional dog walker finds her Afghan hound is beyond even her control, and pulls her over so badly that she has ended up with a broken leg. Graeme Hall comes to her aid, helping her to become more confident in dealing with the determined dog. Other clients include a rescue dog who has been sent back by countless owners and a spaniel who is obsessed with chasing her own shadow

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben meets a family that has created a new home in the Devon countryside - but having had to fight to be allowed to build on the land, they now find peace hard to adjust to. Nevertheless, they continue in their drive to be as self-sufficient as possible. However, Ben spots a new development in their lives that could present them with their next big challenge

programme.title: Everest: Conquering the Death Zone
programme.desc: The stories of mountaineers who have reached the summit of the world's tallest mountain, including a look at the dangers of the low oxygen levels at high altitudes. The programme examines the factors that allowed Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay's 1953 expedition to succeed where so many had failed, and reveals how the Sherpas, who work as porters and guides for expeditions, have a physiology uniquely adapted to surviving at high altitudes

programme.title: Snowtrapped: Can't Get Out!
programme.desc: From avalanches to in-car burials, this documentary tells seven stories of people who became trapped by snow. Real-life footage brings to life how some survived against both the odds and the elements, how some were found despite having no means of communicating with the outside world, and how others were not so lucky. Including film from the helmet camera of skier Kristoffer Carlsson, who was buried alive under two metres of snow in Verbier, Switzerland, after triggering an avalanche

programme.title: Police Code Zero: Officer Under Attack
programme.desc: On Anglesey, PC Mark Lee fights for his life after a driver deliberately runs him down and leaves him for dead, while a Wiltshire officer tries to persuade a violent man to hand over his distressed baby. In Liverpool, a terrified officer issues an urgent call for help while hanging out of the passenger side of his own police car - the result of a criminal in handcuffs stealing trying to use it as a getaway vehicle

programme.desc: Interactive gambling

programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Part one of two. A revelation from Rachel prompts the pals to ponder what might have been if their lives had taken different courses. Comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston and Courteney Cox

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The second episode showing what could have happened to the gang members had their lives taken different paths. Carol and Ross decide to spice up their marriage, and Rachel catches Barry being unfaithful. Comedy, with David Schwimmer and Jennifer Aniston

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: Julian Norton is called out to a three-day-old calf that has collapsed. He suspects a bacterial infection and tells farmer Stuart that it could be contagious. Julian also revisits Elsie, a rare-breed pig who needs a pedicure, but catching her is easier said than done. And farmer Roly brings Peter Wright a newborn lamb with a mysterious lump on its head

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary

programme.title: Divine Designs
programme.desc: Paul Binski, Professor of the History of Medieval Art at Cambridge University, visits Chelsea's Gothic-revival Holy Trinity Church designed by John Dando Sedding, and William Lethaby's thatched All Saints' Church in Brockhampton, Herefordshire, which boasts fittings by William Morris and Edward Burne-Jones of the Arts and Crafts movement

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Property expert Ann Maurice offers advice to a divorcing couple trying to sell their three-bedroom Victorian semi in Camberley, Surrey. The main problems are smelly furniture, unfinished DIY jobs and a caravan blocking the driveway, so the team gives the place a lick of paint and a clean

programme.title: Edward Scissorhands (1990)
programme.desc: A reclusive genius creates a boy but dies before completing him, leaving his brainchild with scissor-like blades in place of hands. A kindly woman from a nearby suburb takes pity on the lonely figure and welcomes him into her home. He quickly becomes a hit in the neighbourhood - but then his new friends start to turn against him. Tim Burton's fantasy, starring Johnny Depp, Winona Ryder, Dianne Wiest and Vincent Price

programme.title: The Book of Life (2014)
programme.desc: A man who longs to be a musician instead of a bullfighter like his father has to compete with a childhood friend when they fall for the same woman. Their love triangle catches the eye of the spirits of the dead, leading him on a journey through their bizarre world. Animated adventure, with the voices of Diego Luna, Zoe Saldana and Channing Tatum

programme.title: Bee Movie (2007)
programme.desc: A bee wearies of a life dedicated to producing honey and decides to leave his hive. During his foray into the wider world, he befriends a florist. However, when he realises what becomes of the honey his species toils so hard to make, he resolves to sue the entire human race for stealing from his fellow creatures. Animated comedy, with the voices of Jerry Seinfeld, Renee Zellweger and Chris Rock

programme.title: The Pink Panther Strikes Again (1976)
programme.desc: Inspector Clouseau fends off a murder syndicate led by his former police chief Dreyfus, who has been driven insane by his hatred for the bungling detective. However, doing away with the lucky lawman proves harder than anticipated - even for the world's best assassins. Comedy sequel, starring Peter Sellers, Herbert Lom, Lesley-Anne Down, Burt Kwouk and Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: Behind Enemy Lines (2001)
programme.desc: A reconnaissance pilot is shot down and becomes stranded in enemy territory after filming evidence of a genocidal massacre by Serb militia in Bosnia. The naval air commander for Nato forces in the Balkans defies orders by mounting an unauthorised mission to rescue him, while the airman faces a battle to stay alive as he is pursued by paramilitary forces. Bosnian War drama, starring Gene Hackman, Owen Wilson, Gabriel Macht and Olek Krupa

programme.title: Gifted (2017)
programme.desc: Single man Frank Adler attempts to raise his child prodigy niece, Mary. However, his plans for a normal school life for the youngster are foiled when the youngster's mathematical abilities come to the attention of Frank's formidable mother, Evelyn. Drama, starring Chris Evans, Lindsay Duncan and Mckenna Grace

programme.title: Bad Neighbours 2 (2016)
programme.desc: A couple who have finally rid themselves of the college fraternity next door become stuck with even worse new neighbours in the shape of a raucous college sorority. In desperation, they turn to their old enemies in getting rid of the new arrivals. Comedy, starring Seth Rogen, Zac Efron, Rose Byrne and Chloe Grace Moretz

programme.title: Secrets & Lies (1996)
programme.desc: A successful black optician learns she is adopted and goes in search of her birth mother, who turns out to be a working class white woman, estranged from her brother and struggling to connect with her teenage daughter. The reunited mother and child soon become close friends, but tensions rise at a family gathering. Mike Leigh's drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Marianne Jean-Baptiste and Timothy Spall

programme.title: Up For It?
programme.desc: Ervin dreams of being a fashion designer. Macie shows off her football skills. Plus, sketches and visual vernacular performances

programme.title: Gunpoint (1966)
programme.desc: A sheriff tries to stop a train robbery, but is shot by his own treacherous deputy. Recovering from his wounds, he assembles a posse to bring the outlaws to justice, acquiring an uneasy ally in a saloon owner in love with a dancehall singer the fugitives have taken hostage. Western, starring Audie Murphy and Joan Stanley

programme.title: Sea of Sand (1958)
programme.desc: Allied troops fighting in North Africa in 1943 are redeployed as saboteurs behind enemy lines to blow up Rommel's desert fuel dumps. A clash of personalities between officers initially causes problems, but the men overcome their differences to work together. Second World War adventure, starring Richard Attenborough, John Gregson, Michael Craig and Vincent Ball

programme.title: 12 Angry Men (1957)
programme.desc: Twelve jurors convene to decide the verdict in a murder trial. While 11 of the group are thoroughly convinced the teenage defendant is guilty, one lone voice of reason refuses to cave in to pressure and passionately argues the case for his innocence. Sidney Lumet's legal drama, starring Henry Fonda, Lee J Cobb, Ed Begley, Martin Balsam and Jack Klugman

programme.title: The Man from Laramie (1955)
programme.desc: A cavalry officer determines to avenge the death of his brother, but rather than target the Apaches who killed him, he vows to go after the gang who supplied them with their weapons - leading to conflict with the sadistic son of an elderly rancher. Anthony Mann's Western, starring James Stewart, Arthur Kennedy, Donald Crisp and Cathy O'Donnell

programme.title: Me and Earl and the Dying Girl (2015)
programme.desc: An awkward teenager shuns all kinds of social interaction, apart from making films with his best friend. His well-meaning mother urges him to spend time with a girl who has been diagnosed with leukaemia, and they unexpectedly develop a firm friendship. However, his life is changed forever as her condition gets worse. Comedy drama based on Jesse Andrews' novel, starring Thomas Mann, RJ Cyler and Olivia Cooke

programme.title: Atomic Blonde (2017)
programme.desc: MI6 operative Lorraine Broughton is sent to Berlin before the fall of the wall to investigate the murder of a fellow spy and recover a missing list of double agents. The city is in a dangerously unstable state, and she finds herself targetted by multiple factions plotting to exploit the uncertain future for their own ruthless ends. Thriller, starring Charlize Theron, James McAvoy and John Goodman

programme.title: Brotherhood (2016)
programme.desc: Sam Peel is now married to lawyer Kayla, with whom he's raising two children, and is determined to stay out of trouble, but he is blackmailed into returning to the criminal underworld. Drama sequel concluding the Hood trilogy, written, directed by and starring Noel Clarke

programme.title: Nico, 1988 (2017)
programme.desc: Biopic about the German singer who found fame with the Velvet Underground in the 1960s, following her on tour with her band in the 1980s as she struggles with heroin addiction. Starring Trine Dyrholm, with John Gordon Sinclair and Anamaria Marinca

programme.title: The Cassandra Crossing (1976)
programme.desc: A fleeing terrorist infects transcontinental rail passengers with a deadly virus - prompting the authorities to consider re-routing the train across a dangerously unstable bridge, hoping it will collapse and kill everyone on board to prevent the contagion spreading. Disaster epic, starring Burt Lancaster, Richard Harris, Martin Sheen and Sophia Loren

programme.title: The Way to the Stars (1945)
programme.desc: A naive young pilot is assigned to a British bomber base during the Second World War, and is involved in the opening skirmishes of the Battle of Britain. He encounters the first wave of American recruits to the Allies, and falls for a local girl - but when his squadron leader is killed leaving a wife and child behind, he questions whether he can afford to marry. Drama, starring John Mills and Michael Redgrave

programme.title: Legend of the Lost (1957)
programme.desc: A grizzled adventurer living in Timbuktu is hired to escort a mysterious explorer across the Sahara desert in search of a fabled lost city. A local woman joins the men on their quest, but when the trio reach their destination the explorer reveals his plan to betray his companions and leave them high and dry. Adventure, starring John Wayne, Sophia Loren and Rossano Brazzi

programme.title: Bridge of Spies (2015)
programme.desc: A lawyer in 1950s New York is hired to represent a Soviet spy in a showcase trial designed to demonstrate the fairness of the US justice system. In the face of public condemnation, he argues against the death penalty, a stance that years later leads to his involvement in an exchange of prisoners in East Germany. Steven Spielberg's fact-based Cold War drama, starring Tom Hanks and an Oscar-winning Mark Rylance

programme.title: Unsane (2018)
programme.desc: A young woman is involuntarily committed to a psychiatric institute, where she is confronted by her greatest fear - but is it real or part of her delusion? Steven Soderbergh's horror, starring Claire Foy, Joshua Leonard and Jay Pharoah

programme.title: Dark River (2017)
programme.desc: After receiving news of her father's death, travelling sheep-shearer Alice tentatively decides to return to the dilapidated family house of her childhood in muddy North Yorkshire for the first time in 15 years, hoping to claim the tenancy to the family farm she believes is rightfully hers. Period drama, starring Ruth Wilson, Mark Stanley and Sean Bean

programme.title: American Honey (2016)
programme.desc: A poor teenager leaves home and hits the road with a crew of magazine sellers. She gets caught up in a whirlwind of hard partying, law bending and young love while crossing the Midwest United States. Drama, starring Sasha Lane, Shia LaBeouf and Riley Keough

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Omnibus. Felix, Warren and Brody await their arrest after the news about Cormac. Meanwhile, Cher gets into trouble with Sylver and takes her anger out on Romeo

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island. The no-nonsense chef struggles to persuade hot-headed owner Abby to make his suggested changes, and a confrontation results in him being thrown out of the building in the middle of a dinner service. The eatery's co-owner and financier begs Gordon to return, but his success hinges on his ability to win Abby over

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the Prohibition Grille in Everett, Washington, where he meets belly dancer-turned-restaurateur Rishi Brown, who is making all the wrong moves with her business. Distracted from the task of improving her menu by performing for customers, she's oblivious to the disastrous dishes being served from her own kitchen

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The remaining couples have been through the trials and tribulations of the experiment and now face the ultimate test of their relationship - the final vows

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The last couple say their final vows - but with their relationship in turmoil, will they go through with it?

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Six weeks after the end of the realty experiment, all 24 participants reunite for the very last dinner party

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Nashville, Tennessee, the spiritual home of country music, but his visit begins on a sour note at Chappy's Cajun restaurant. Chef John Chapman's careless methods have damaged his reputation, but he blames the local clientele rather than his less-than-appetising food and disturbing kitchen practices. Can Gordon get through to this stubborn owner and save the eatery from disaster?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Scottsdale, Arizona, where he meets husband-and-wife bistro owners Samy and Amy Bouzaglo, who reveal a shocking contempt for customer feedback. The chef faces the biggest challenge of the series to save Amy's Baking Company, but can he get through to this defiant pair?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard becomes convinced he has met his soulmate in the form of surgeon Stephanie Barnett, but he fails to realise she is far more interested in Leonard. Meanwhile, Sheldon and Raj argue about what to watch on TV. Sara Rue guest stars

programme.title: Gogglesprogs
programme.desc: Young TV viewers give their honest, inimitable and hilarious take on The Crystal Maze Celebrity Special, The Great Pottery Throw Down and classic movie The Jungle Book

programme.title: Teen First Dates
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix welcomes young people aged between 16 and 19 to a restaurant in Manchester, where the teenagers go on blind dates. In the first edition, flamboyant art student Archie seeks a muse, and his love of camp culture hits all the right notes with 19-year-old musician Eden

programme.title: Eddie the Eagle (2016)
programme.desc: Biopic of British sporting underdog Eddie Edwards. Determined to fulfil his dreams of becoming an Olympic athlete, Edwards trains as a ski jumper - a field in which Britain had no other competitors. He secures a place in the 1988 Winter Olympics, where his can-do attitude makes him a media sensation in the face of a losing streak. Starring Taron Egerton, Hugh Jackman and Christopher Walken

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Shows including Countdown, Trevor McDonald's Indian Train Adventure, Titanic, First Dates and Serengeti come under the critical gaze of famous faces including Jessie J, Jamal Edwards, Martin and Roman Kemp, Rylan Clark-Neal, Denise Van Outen, Oti Mabuse, Chris Eubank Sr, Chris Eubank Jr, Stanley Johnson, Georgia 'Toff' Toffolo, Laurence and Emilia Fox, Gyles Brandreth, Sheila Hancock, Emily Atack, Kate Robbins, Nick Grimshaw and Olly Alexander

programme.title: Temptation Island
programme.desc: Four British couples have their relationships put to the test on an island filled with sexy singles. Here, the couples ponder the consequences of their actions on the island

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies presents another assortment of absurd assignments, as Alex Horne tries to ignore Richard Herring hiding menacingly in a bush, Katherine Parkinson pays a visit to the fridge, Mawaan Rizwan steals a cup of tea and Johnny Vegas takes a tumble

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The households' reactions to September 2018 programmes including This Morning, Naked Attraction, The X Factor, Bodyguard, Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain, Panorama: Inside No 10: Deal or No Deal?, Stacey Dooley: Face to Face with Armageddon, and The Circle

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is delighted that Leonard has finally managed to find a girlfriend who meets his high standards, but his over-exuberance looks set to cause problems. Jim Parsons and Johnny Galecki star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

With guest star Michael Trucco (Battlestar Galactica, One Tree Hill)

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: When self-proclaimed ladies' man Jayden, 20, meets childminder Isabel, 19, things go well between them - until they realise they have a mutual friend. Sarah, 44, has been deaf since birth, and wants to find love with a hearing man, so she's relieved to find 47-year-old estate agent John is easy to lip read. Eternal romantic Tim returns to the restaurant, and his date, Sarah, shares his passion for bad jokes, exploding high fives and limericks. Disco-loving personal trainer Ellis, 47, is pleased when 53-year-old hairdresser Gary turns up, and his touching life story makes her warm to him even more. Meanwhile, Ryan, 33, has recently become single after an 11-year relationship, and turns to alcohol to help him relax

programme.title: Malcolm in the Middle

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.desc: Peralta and Diaz work and rework a crime scene in their efforts to solve a difficult murder case that is clouded by confusing evidence. However, their sympathy for the mourning mother of the victim leads them to make unrealistic promises to her

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect attends a carol service in a 130-year-old flat-pack tin church, while Will Hardie visits a very different chapel that has been transformed into a luxury home. The duo help an ice carver create a winter wonderland-themed bar in Liverpool, and the programme catches up with some of George's favourite builds of the year. There are also a few Christmas drinks in the garden of Joel Bird, winner of the Shed of the Year prize

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: Architect Chris Dyson helps Wendy and Allan in Colchester, who are desperate to transform their horrible-looking 1960s home into something more modern. Adamant that the house needs a drastic makeover externally and a major reconfiguration internally, can Chris satisfy their high expectations while sticking to their £150,000 budget?

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George Clarke gets to work on a 300-year-old house in Broadstairs, which was once a school and a retirement community, leaving it with a few quirky features - not least a row of three toilets. For inspiration, he visits the Royal Pavilion in Brighton. He also helps a couple make the most of the tiny living room in their 1930s south London bungalow

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Surrey, five cooks from the Woking and Weybridge area battle to win the culinary contest's £1,000 prize. The first to host is Vincenzo Ferlita, who works in an ice-cream van. Staying true to his Italian and Sicilian roots, he hopes that his wealth of family recipes will impress his guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Canadian housewife and craft-maker Sarah Maynard is second to play host in the Woking and Weybridge area, and hopes to impress her guests by serving up a three-course Turkish feast. However, the lavish spread needs a lot of preparation time, and she must hope it turns out as presentable as she plans it to be

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Riot policeman Steve Carvill hosts the third dinner party in Woking and Weybridge in Surrey, and hopes that a night of fine food and fun will impress his guests. He takes no prisoners with his menu, rustling up home-made pasta and white chocolate panna cotta from scratch for his four unsuspecting diners, who are expecting fast food rather than fine dining

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the turn of gardener Sara Gilding on the fourth night in Woking and Weybridge in Surrey, but it is her first time hosting a dinner party in more than 20 years. Guest Vincenzo Ferlita is not looking forward to the evening, while Steve Carvill hopes he will get some respite from Sara's flirtatious advances

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Children's entertainer Kelly Cummins hosts the final party from Woking and Weybridge in Surrey, and the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced. The guests keep their fingers crossed that dog-loving Sara Gilding does not return to her critical ways, while a bout of flatulence provides some comic relief

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Newton House in Knaresborough, North Yorkshire, where Denise Carter and Jackie Fennell's traditional B&B focuses on organic, free-range breakfasts. Over dinner, guest Marianne Cammack and Deina Thomas from dominate the conversation, riling up some of the others, while Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson think the morning meal is all way too fancy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to the Highlands Pub in Blackpool, where managers Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson are all about value for money. However, Sean and Kathryn are horrified by what they find in their room, while Marianne and Deina think that theirs needs the female touch. In the afternoon the group competes in a basketball challenge at the amusement arcades, where the quiet hosts come out of their shells

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the White House in Brighton for the third day of the competition, where hosts Sean Edlin and Kathryn Turnbull run a laid-back seaside B&B. Denise and Jackie think the rooms are not well equipped and Marianne finds them clinical, while Mike and Neal are unsure about the price. Tensions are temporarily alleviated by a visit to the famous Theatre Royal, but Marianne and Deina continue to cause trouble at dinner. In the morning, a locally sourced breakfast impresses some of the guests, but the group must decide whether they are getting value for money

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wakes Hall Lodges near Colchester is the fourth and final visit of the week, where Neal and Mike are wowed by the five-star self-catering accommodation, complete with hot tubs and lakeside views. The others aren't so sure about the 'luxury' breakfast hamper, however, especially as it means cooking their own. An afternoon of dog agility gives manager Marianne Cammack the chance to demonstrate her authority, although over dinner, her domineering conversation causes Jackie to finally snap

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Mike and Neal from the Highlands Pub challenge the comments that they were considered dirty and lacking in facilities, while Sean and Kathryn from the White House are left smarting after suffering large underpayments for what they think are minor issues. Eventually, the winner is finally revealed

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel decide they will live as self-sufficiently as they can while restoring the chateau, and invest in some chickens to provide them with eggs for breakfast every morning. However, they still have countless problems to deal with, and have only £20,000 left to spend on the project. The chateau is still without either heating or water, while the 59 windows are crumbling. While Dick's renovations fall behind schedule, Angel is in her element coming up with ideas for what the finished building will look like

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree only have a few weeks to prepare for their wedding, but the chateau is nowhere near ready to receive the 200 guests they have invited and finding reliable, affordable workers is proving difficult. Dick oversees the damming of the moat and the installation of a new, eco-friendly sewage system, while Angel turns her attention to giving the rooms a distinctive decor

programme.title: A Taste of Italy
programme.desc: Nisha Katona explores the city of Sulmona in Abruzzo, uncovering the local specialties of the region and using their ingredients to create delicious lamb kebabs

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Documentary following the stories of three patients being treated for cardiac problems at St George's Hospital, south-west London. Corey has suffered cardiac arrest while playing ice hockey, prompting doctors to keep the 24-year-old in an induced coma to prevent further damage to his brain. At almost 60, Angie has a history of heart disease and her heart has been beating at over 150 for eight hours, risking stroke and other complications. Doctors also carry out tests on 49-year-old Steve to determine the cause of his dangerously high heart-rate

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: An hour in the life of paramedics across the country

programme.title: Surviving a Serial Killer
programme.desc: The personal stories of people who have survived face-to-face meetings with murderers. The first edition features Rhonda Stapley, who was attacked by Ted Bundy

programme.title: Murder in the Outback: The Falconio and Lees Mystery
programme.desc: Documentary examining the killing of British tourist Peter Falconio in Australia's Northern Territory in July 2001, exploring the details and questions that surround this infamous case. The concluding episode examines how DNA found on Joanne Lees' shirt and the couple's Kombi van were decisive in the murder trial of Bradley Murdoch, but even with this evidence, there are serious questions still to be answered in this controversial case

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Linden, 15, is brought to A&E having badly cut his leg while back-flipping off a shed roof onto a trampoline. As well as calling an ambulance, his friends filmed the accident on their mobile phones and happily show the clip to the staff at King's. Junior doctor Sammy looks after 88-year-old Panayiota, who has been referred to hospital with breathing difficulties. Originally from Cyprus, the patient speaks very little English and Sammy has to contact a translator to help with the diagnosis and treatment. Meanwhile, 55-year-old lorry driver Antony has lost all feeling in his left leg, and an ultrasound scan reveals he needs emergency surgery on a blocked artery to save his limb

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: An hour in the life of paramedics across the country

programme.title: Surviving a Serial Killer
programme.desc: The personal stories of people who have survived face-to-face meetings with murderers. The first edition features Rhonda Stapley, who was attacked by Ted Bundy

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The priests organise a raffle to raise funds to repair their leaky roof, planning to award a car as the prize. Unfortunately, Ted accidentally wrecks it before the grand draw takes place, and embarks on a frantic search for a replacement. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon play the Craggy Island clerics

programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp teams up with potter Richard Miller to help Ellie and Nick Harrington - who have no practical DIY skills - create plates to complement their 1930s-style dining room in Tunbridge Wells, Kent

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps husband and wife George and Lennox Thomas find their ideal holiday home in and around the Spanish area of Marina Almeria to escape their busy London lives

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel to East Sussex in their hunt to discover valuable items 'lost' in people's homes, with their finds including some Victorian street lamps

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Experts Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel Britain to find, fix and flog unwanted possessions on behalf of their owners, turning items into hard cash. A butter churn, a slot machine and a set of print trays are among the finds in this edition

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree make plans to host weddings in the grounds of their propery, while looking ahead to keeping livestock and growing their own crops. Dick takes steps to generate solar power during a hot summer, and turns an old oil tank into a pig roaster

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets a couple who cannot agree on what their dream home should look like. While midwife Liz likes traditional houses, accountant Craig favours contemporary styles, and when the build veers towards the modern, Liz struggles to stay engaged. However, Charlie is worried that the pair are making some big mistakes when it comes to the building's eco-features

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Hero in Burnham Overy Staithe, Norfolk, which is run by two friends who quit university to follow their dream of becoming hospitality entrepreneurs

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Cleasewood Guest House in Great Yarmouth, Norfolk, a labour of love for rookie B&B owners Debbie and Arthur Callaghan

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at Peel Hey on the Wirral, where Helen Holmes and Suzanne Hill hope their years of experience will give them the edge over their rivals

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Shoukry Rozek welcomes his rivals to Four Saints Brig y Don in Llandudno, north Wales, where the sea views impress some guests, although the buffet breakfast proves controversial

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners are meeting for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. The Hero is first, and hosts Harry and Rowan Glennie want to know why their female guests thought their rooms were too boyish to cut the mustard

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more items to fix up and sell on, visiting Windsor to meet Pete and Phil, who rent out cars to the film and TV business. Henry finds an Austrian moped which is missing an engine, while Simon picks a fibreglass suit of armour

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Young meerkat couple Beagle and Huskie become parents for the first time, and spider monkey Kiara is finding it increasingly difficult to bring up her first daughter, Laura. Meanwhile, vets decide to examine painted dog K'mana's ankle because of an old injury, leaving her partner Ville alone to cope with their seven adolescent puppies

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Fresh from the success of hosting a three-day wedding at the chateau, Dick and Angel make business plans for the grounds, intending to charge people to fish in the moat and forage in the woods. Angel starts work on a second guest suite, but also wants to install an elevator, which pushes Dick's engineering skills to the limit

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of two architects who are planning to build an eco-friendly subterranean house underneath a ruined 300-year-old barn in the Cotswolds. In one of the most challenging projects seen on the programme, Helen and Chris Seymour Smith build a steel frame around the barn while they excavate underneath, aiming to create a home that is airtight and needs no heating

programme.title: Darcey Bussell's Wild Coasts of Scotland
programme.desc: Darcey takes an emotional trip to Bute, where for the very first time she visits the house where her grandfather spent so many childhood summers

programme.title: Coastal Railways with Julie Walters
programme.desc: The actress travels on the famous Great Western Railway to Cornwall. She recalls childhood holidays when she stops at Torquay before heading to a supposedly haunted castle at Kingswear. She also uncovers tales of smuggling and joins members of the RNLI on a practice run before visiting an unusual cake shop in Penzance

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: It's a busy Mother's Day at south London's King's College Hospital as 23-year-old builder Jamie is rushed in by paramedics having fallen from the roof of a three-storey building, landing on a brick wall. The team performs an emergency ultrasound to determine whether he has suffered internal injuries or fractured his ribs and spine, and spot a punctured lung that needs to be reinflated. Four-month-old Weston has been brought to paediatric A&E after suffering from diarrhoea and vomiting for more than 24 hours, while doctors suspect David, 74, has had a stroke. Junior doctor Matt looks after 80-year-old Daphne, who has dislocated her replacement hip

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jon Richardson takes on guest captain Johnny Vegas in another round of words-and-numbers chaos. Their team-mates are Alex Brooker and Sara Pascoe, while Claudia Winkleman and Rob Beckett stand in for Rachel Riley on letters-and-numbers duty, and Tom Allen is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent. Hosted by Jimmy Carr

programme.title: Father Ted

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien repurposes some old suitcases, while Henry Cole gives an old dresser some serious bling

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the Seawood Hotel in Lynton, Devon, where owners Martin and Kyle Fletcher hope to win over their guests with their high standards

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Gooseford Farm B&B in Dartmoor, Devon, where Mary Courtier looks after the B&B and husband Julian takes care of the farm

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants head to Trevarrian Lodge in Newquay, Cornwall, where Jean Hegarty and her husband - professional chef Dec - are hoping the food will win their guests over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Rivendell Outdoor Club, a clothing-optional resort in Wimborne, Dorset, managed by couple Kerry Murphy and Andy Gautry. On arrival not all the contestants are happy to let it all hang out but a warm welcome from the hosts soon puts them all at ease

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: As the B&B owners meet for the final time, things get heated when Martin and Kyle want to know why Jean and Dec gave their dressing gowns a thumbs down. Mary and Julian are keen to understand why the price wasn't right for Jean and Dec, while Kerry and Andy want to know why they got a low hosting score

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo head for Crewe to meet rock and roller Billy, who lives in a house that is frozen in time in the 1950s

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: A power struggle unfolds as young chimp Carlos challenges dominant male Dylan, and Asian short-clawed otter Wallace is struck down by a mystery illness. Meanwhile, bush dog Franco's pack has stopped growing after his most recent litter died, and keepers hope that moving one of the breeding females to another zoo might solve the problem

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Two-and-a-half years have passed since Dick and Angel Strawbridge swapped their flat in Southend for a 19th-century chateau in France costing only £280,000, and in that time, the pair have worked to transform the derelict property into a fairytale castle. However, around half of the 45 rooms are yet to receive adequate attention, the couple's fledgling events business demands attention, and the outbuildings are still in need of conversion

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of Simon and Jill Bennett as they try to restore two timber-framed barns in Suffolk. The couple found out that one of the buildings is among the smallest surviving medieval guildhalls in the country, and while Kevin advises them to conserve it in its present state, Simon is determined to restore it to its former glory

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge attempt to renovate the orangery with the help of a roofing expert and some pre-loved slate, all the way from Wales. They also discover some unexpected history beneath their feet, and Angel finds two old daybeds in need of upcycling

programme.title: When Cruises Go Wrong
programme.desc: In a typical tourist season, around two million British people will take to the seas for the holiday of a lifetime promising class, elegance and refinement. However, as this documentary reveals, it's not always plain sailing. Cameras capture the downside of cruising, from sickness and crime to terrifying weather, collisions and emergency evacuations

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The trauma team at St George's Hospital in south-west London fights to save the life of a young woman involved in a motorbike accident, which has completely severed her right leg below the knee. An 11-year-old boy is brought in after having a prolonged epileptic seizure, and a 19-year-old arrives with a sewing needle stuck in her foot, but she seems more keen on taking advice from her mum in Bulgaria than from the doctor

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

Rick explores a city of many identities, boasting a young student population apparently hungry for dishes that reflect the freshest of seafood. programme.desc: A major service disruption hits Paddington on Meet the Manager day, and there is a look at Brunel's last engineering masterpiece, the Royal Albert Bridge, which spans the River Tamar and connects Devon and Cornwall. programme.desc: Gruber finishes the paintings and the spy camera sent from London has fallen into a vineyard where the peasants are refusing to work for fear of being shot

Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following the work of the train operating company's staff along the route between London, south-west England and south Wales. The first edition focuses on problems caused by last winter's storms, with the worst weather for a century battering the nation and pushing employees and passengers to the limit. Network Rail's Steve Hawkins and his engineers have their work cut out after the line at Dawlish in Devon is washed away, while Dave Slater and his team at Network Control have to battle the elements to keep trains running as storm after storm floods the network

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: As rugby union's Six Nations Championship kicks off in Cardiff, train manager Jodie Edwards and her team try to get thousands of fans home after the match. Penzance in Cornwall remains shut off from the rest of the network as engineers work around the clock to get services running through Dawlish, where the line was washed away, while new duty manager Joe has his hands full on his first Saturday night shift at London's Paddington station when the last Wales-bound train of the evening has a problem

programme.title: History Hunters
programme.desc: Dave and Steve spot something special at auction, but they're not the only ones on the trail of this First World War pilot's logbook

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of Hampton Court Palace, which started out life as a Tudor palace in 1515 and is famous for being the home of King Henry VIII

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of Schönbrunn Palace in Austria, which is located in a 500-acre park and includes an orangery, palm house and one of Europe's oldest zoos

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The next part of Michael Portillo's journey from London to Leeds begins in Northampton, where Victorian methods have been used to make shoes for more than 130 years. The ex-politician then heads to Warwickshire, learning about the legacy of Dr Thomas Arnold at Rugby School and finding out how Coventry's craftsmen learned to adapt to survive, before ending this leg of his trip in Nuneaton

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the next stage of his journey to Leeds, Michael Portillo sets off in Leicester, where he finds out about the hunt for Richard III's remains. Michael works his passage on the Great Central Railway from Rothley to Loughborough, where he learns about a family that has been casting bells in the town since 1839

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second of two episodes from Balmoral, where she takes a tour of the grounds in a 1953 land rover and views archive footage of the royal family. Valuables brought in to be viewed include a collection of medals from both World Wars presented to a man who also rescued survivors from the Titanic, an art nouveau mirror and a 16th-century bottle that survived being unearthed by a digger

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: How engineers have built railways across mountains, including tunnels through the Swiss Alps and an Australian track that runs up a near-vertical cliff face

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: The search for looted gold hidden by the Nazis in North Africa. Plus, how the Russian army used dogs as a secret weapon and a mysterious message just before the Pearl Harbor attack

programme.title: The World at War

Elsewhere in Europe, the Nazi war machine cut a swathe of destruction. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey, Michael Portillo rediscovers a once-famous poet in Nottingham and travels on a railway line resurrected by popular demand after falling victim to Beeching's cuts. He finds out how Doncaster rail workers shaped British political history, before reaching his final destination of Leeds, where he auditions at Britain's oldest continuously working music hall

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the first leg of a journey from Southampton to Wolverhampton, beginning by being taught how to set a table aboard the luxury liner Queen Elizabeth. In the village of Netley he finds the remains of an extensive military hospital built by order of Queen Victoria, and ends his travels in Basingstoke, where he learns out about a pitched battle between townspeople and the Salvation Army

programme.title: World War Two From Above
programme.desc: Experts uncover a WWII German trench system revealing a daring commando raid to capture it. And, the sunken remains of a tank show how dangerous preparations for D-Day were

programme.title: War Factories

programme.title: The Two Ronnies

programme.title: Viking Dead
programme.desc: Experts analyse the content of Viking burial sites to learn more about Norse culture, beginning with the mass grave of a raiding party, which dates back to Roman times

programme.title: Viking Dead
programme.desc: Archaeologists investigate the discovery of a mass grave in Dorset, and try to determine if the bodies are the remains of Vikings

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Cheltenham Spa station manager Richard Morrish braces himself for a hectic four days with the two-platform station used by more than 60,000 horse racing fans during the Cheltenham Festival. Just as the mainline at Dawlish is about to be reopened after being washed away in the winter storms, a crack is discovered in the sandstone cliffs two miles down the line and a landslide threatens to engulf the tracks. Conductor Jameil and driver Gary have to react quickly on the Reading to Gatwick service when a pedestrian takes a perilous short cut

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: As the deadline approaches for the reopening of the mainline at Dawlish, a 300-strong army of engineers works 24 hours a day to keep to the timetable. At London's Paddington station, a train with a Pullman silver service dining car is being prepared, with the onboard team pulling out all the stops to impress food writer Regula Ysewijn, who is researching an article. Elsewhere, North Cotswolds station manager Theresa - known as the 'smiling tiger' for the way she runs her stations firmly and with great care and dedication - keeps a watchful eye on the line between Oxford and Hereford, which carries the highest proportion of first-class ticket holders on the network

programme.title: History Hunters

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a Christmas special from Lyme Park in Cheshire, where she and toy expert Will Farmer look back at children's Christmas presents dating back to Edwardian times. The programme features a cup and saucer once owned by the Queen, a pram from Buckingham Palace, and a dagger believed to have been owned by Charles I. programme.desc: Unusual rail networks designed to traverse water, including the Indian Ocean, the earthquake zones of the Bosphorus Strait and the sea cliffs of the Italian Riviera

programme.title: The World at War

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Hampshire and Berkshire, as he continues his journey to Wolverhampton. His stop-off points include Stratfield Saye House, the stately home bequeathed by the nation to Arthur Wellesley, 1st Duke of Wellington, in 1817. He then heads to Aldershot Garrison, where he is put through his paces under military instruction. At St Michael's Abbey in Farnborough, Michael visits the tomb of French emperor Napoleon III and his family, before ending in Crowthorne, Berkshire, the home of Broadmoor high-security psychiatric hospital

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The next part of Michael Portillo's journey from Southampton to Wolverhampton begins in Wokingham, Berkshire, where the former politician finds out how demand from a growing number of rail commuters fuelled the development of the modern printing press. He then learns about a Tudor businessman who manufactured cloth in enormous volumes in Newbury, and ends this leg of his trip in Bradford on Avon, Wiltshire. Here, a local manufacturer describes how his Victorian forebears were the first in Britain to be licensed to vulcanise rubber

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Mother-of-three Sheila wants a family friendly property in the thriving resort of Benalmadena on the Costa del Sol, with its sunny beaches and lively atmosphere. With a budget of £135,000 and specifying the need for sea views, Danni Menzies sets out to deliver the dream place

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas shows five properties to Greater Manchester couple Sandra and Robert, who want a holiday home on Spain's Costa Blanca with plenty of storage space for their bikes and located within a short walk to the beach

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Former How Clean is Your House? presenter Kim Woodburn and her husband Pete decide they would like to take ownership of a holiday home on the Costa del Sol, so Jonnie Irwin takes them to view five penthouses that meet their £320,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Angela and Michael Sidebotham from Lincolnshire are ready to fulfil their life-long dreams of buying a home in Davenport, Florida, where they plan to spend half of each year. With a budget of £160,000, Sara Damergi shows the couple five properties that meet their requirements

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Chris and Dan Boettcher from Bedfordshire find a holiday home in the Axarquia region of Spain's Costa del Sol on their budget of £70,000. They view five properties, hoping one of them will be the retreat they can escape to with their family without too much prior planning

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: First of a two-part special in which Gordon Ramsay heads to the Burger Kitchen in Los Angeles, where tensions are running high because owner Alan Saffron has spent his son's entire inheritance on the struggling restaurant. To make matters worse, the kitchen is run by a chef called David Blaine who shares nothing of his famous namesake's magic when it comes to cooking - and whose threatening behaviour regularly gets him into trouble

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Part two of two. The chef continues his quest to transform the fortunes of the Burger Kitchen in Los Angeles, where tensions were running high because owner Alan Saffron had spent his son's entire inheritance on the struggling restaurant. Alan gets a wake-up call from internet critics, who he believes are trying to shut him down

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay visits family-run Luigi's in Orange County, California, where lazy shortcuts following the retirement of the owner's father have led the business to suffer. Ramsay faces the daunting task of reuniting a feuding family, before turning around the struggling restaurant, which currently has 126 dishes on the menu

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to the Greek at the Harbour restaurant in southern California, where owner Makis Mikelatos works 17 hours a day, seven days a week, but is unwilling to let his son Ari take the reins. Gordon sets out to get to the bottom of Makis's reluctance to teach his son, and uncovers a shocking family secret

programme.title: Undercover Police: Hunting Paedophiles
programme.desc: Following an officer who assumes a new identity as Oli, a gay 14-year-old boy from Bedfordshire seeking to explore his newfound sexuality on social media platforms. Before long he is contacted by a number of adult men, some of whom continue engaging with him even when he reveals to them that he is a child. As the conversations quickly turn sexual, the team behind the undercover officer race to identify the app users who are trying to arrange to sexually abuse 'Oli'

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An 88-year-old man collapses, and doctors fear she is not getting enough blood to the brain. A law student is treated for a condition that causes his ribs to grow inwardly, and discusses the bullying he has experienced in his life. A bartender is rushed to A&E after a barrel falls on his leg, and reflects on his worries about becoming a father

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: It's the quarter-final, and the remaining potters face a challenge inspired by Native American Acoma pottery that involves firing their pots in cow dung. Judges Rich Miller and Keith Brymer Jones set a tricky second challenge to throw an Alabama ring bottle in this all-American week, but who will secure an all-important place in the semi-final? Siobhan McSweeney hosts

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: The presenter explores the UK's wildest, most remote and beautifully designed holiday homes, accompanied by some of the nation's most fascinating and funny women. This time, Sandi is joined by the comedian, broadcaster and her friend Sindhu Vee to explore some of the best staycations Scotland has to offer. First, the pair visit a private island off the coast of Skye, before checking into a luxury shooting lodge in the Highlands. Finally, they arrive at a truly unique, elliptical shaped home built to celebrate the island views of the Inner Hebrides and the Sound of Mull

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back to some of their favourite properties from the show and offers tips and tricks to help viewers make the best of their homes. As well as catching up with some of the families that previously featured, Kirstie and Phil focus on the layout issues and dilemmas that many of us now face in a very different world. In this episode, the pair focus the best ways to make a budget stretch, as two families with grand renovation plans look to make their money go a long way

programme.title: Lose a Stone in 21 Days with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: Dr Michael Mosley reunites five volunteers, whose health he helped transform in Lose a Stone in 21 Days with Michael Mosley, in a bid to myth-bust some of the common misconceptions around healthy weight management. Using cutting-edge tests and technology to illustrate that the calories our volunteers actually need day to day, could be different to the NHS's recommended daily amounts, the show investigates whether weight loss inevitably leads to putting weight back on again, and explores whether exercise is as helpful as we think. There is also bespoke advice to each volunteer on how to achieve a healthy weight for the long term

programme.title: Grayson's Art Club
programme.desc: Turner prize-winning artist Grayson Perry and Philippa Perry are joined by musician and artist Boy George, who creates an artwork inspired by family, which is this week's theme. Grayson and Boy George then select their favourite public art submissions of the week. Grayson also meets photographer David Bailey, who talks about his passion for painting, and artist Yinka Illori, whose work fuses stories from his British and Nigerian heritage. Plus, a special contribution from guests including Harry Hill

programme.title: Back
programme.desc: Keen for revenge, Stephen and Alison set out on a road trip to track down Andrew's birth mother. But as they get close to achieving their goal, Stephen worries that they may have crossed a line. Alison's newly single mum Wendy is struggling to settle in at the Barleycorn, Geoff faces a dilemma when he is offered a fortune to sell the picturesque burial plot next to his late wife's, and a rescue dog takes a surprising interest in Ellen. Black comedy, starring David Mitchell and Robert Webb

programme.title: Coach Trip

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, be they entertainment juggernauts, hard-hitting documentaries, news reports or gritty dramas

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An 88-year-old man collapses, and doctors fear he is not getting enough blood to the brain. A law student is treated for a condition that causes his ribs to grow inwardly, and discusses the bullying he has experienced in his life. A bartender is rushed to A&E after a barrel falls on his leg, and reflects on his worries about becoming a father

programme.title: Escape to the Wild
programme.desc: Kevin travels to Central America to meet British family-of-five the Atkinsons, who have quit London to live a simpler existence in the rainforests of Belize. As he attempts to understand their motivations for the move, he suffers from the heat, is attacked by swarms of biting insects and shares his mud hut with a large scorpion, but begins to appreciate the rewards of a simple jungle life, one in which they've constructed a two-storey building entirely from recycled rubbish

programme.title: Max Clifford: The Fall of a Tabloid King
Stand Up & Deliver
programme.desc: Five celebrities perform stand-up comedy in support of Stand Up to Cancer, with leading comics mentoring them along the way. David Baddiel coaches broadcaster and former Communard the Rev Richard Coles, Jason Manford is tasked with shepherding Happy Mondays frontman Shaun Ryder, Nick Helm guides Baroness Sayeeda Warsi, Judi Love drills Love Island star and dancer Curtis Pritchard and Zoe Lyons champions former Coronation Street actor Katie McGlynn

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Road Trip
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix embarks on a road trip around some of Europe's most beautiful locations, meeting the Brits who have set up lives abroad. In the first edition, he sets out on a tour of southern Spain's whitewashed villages in Andalucía, where three sets of expats each run beautiful boutique hotels. He meets a couple with a grand plan to host weddings, and another learning on the job. Plus, can a single mum juggle work and family?

programme.title: Secret Safari: Into the Wild
programme.desc: A young cheetah must protect her four cubs after they stray into lion territory. A dominant male hartebeest defends his territory and dwindling herd from predators, and one of Ol Pejeta's smallest but most important inhabitants, the cocktail ant, needs the rangers' help to fight off a full-scale invasion

programme.title: Drawers Off
programme.desc: Jenny Eclair hosts a competition in which amateur artists compete to win a cash prize by creating life portraits of one another as they take it in turns to strip off and strike a special pose for their fellow artists to capture on canvas

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: An Essex couple dream up a new idea to boost income at their campsite in France, and a pair of gite owners reveal whether they made it past a difficult first year

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin assists Joanna and Scott, who met 18 years ago in southern Spain while they were on a golfing trip, and are now searching for a home on the Costa Blanca

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps married couple Ali and Ben Brown from Kent find their ideal property abroad, as she shows them five places on the Spanish island of Gran Canaria

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton meets a London couple who want a holiday home in Mojacar, southern Spain, showing them a townhouse with stunning views for £25,000 less than their budget

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fitness fanatic Claire Wier hosts the first party in Worcestershire and hopes her low-fat menu and pre-dinner exercise routines are enough to secure her the £1,000 prize. However, her guests are disappointed that the evening meal is lacking in cream, fat, oil and butter

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty pageant queen Pippa Langhorne throws the second party in Worcestershire, and hopes to impress her fellow diners with a sophisticated Midsummer Night's Dream-themed event. However, her starter fails to win the approval of shellfish-hating Claire Wier, and Tim Lawson disapproves on the basis of his friendship with fish. Everyone is surprised as the hostess brings out an unusual dessert, and a guest appearance by her singing dog Buddy rounds off a memorable evening

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Publican Tim Lawson hosts the third dinner party in Worcestershire, laying on a country-themed evening featuring a novel take on prawn cocktail and a creamy main course. Chris Sheppard finds himself confronted about his tan, while tension mounts between Pippa Langhorne and Claire Wier, who takes issue with her fellow diner's claims to have a white bread allergy

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Teresa Cox hosts the fourth dinner party challenge from Worcestershire and sets her store on wooing the guests with a lobster starter and an extravagant main course. The menu seems to go down well with the diners, but when the conversation turns to the paranormal there are mixed reactions

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Chris Sheppard hosts the final dinner party in Worcestershire, throwing a black-tie-themed evening. He impresses some of his guests with his home-made sweet chilli sauce and potato towers - although Claire Wier finds the presentation pretentious - before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Part two of two. The chef continues his quest to transform the fortunes of the Burger Kitchen in Los Angeles, where tensions were running high because owner Alan Saffron had spent his son's entire inheritance on the struggling restaurant. Alan gets a wake-up call from internet critics, who he believes are trying to shut him down

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps a couple with £100,000 to spend to relocate to the Charente in western France, where they hope to lead a more relaxed life in the countryside

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Road Trip
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix embarks on a road trip around some of Europe's most beautiful locations, meeting the Brits who have set up lives abroad. In the first edition, he sets out on a tour of southern Spain's whitewashed villages in Andalucía, where three sets of expats each run beautiful boutique hotels. He meets a couple with a grand plan to host weddings, and another learning on the job. Plus, can a single mum juggle work and family?

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke meets an old friend in Norfolk who is planning to turn an old sewage works into a unique chill-out space for his family, complete with a self-supporting dome roof and a circular carp pond. A Midlands father and daughter work together on salvaging a dilapidated childhood treehouse with a budget of just of £300, George and Will find unusual inspiration as they continue building their own treehouse, and in Japan, George visits a breath-taking chapel made of intertwining concrete ribbons

programme.title: Lose a Stone in 21 Days with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: Dr Michael Mosley reunites five volunteers, whose health he helped transform in Lose a Stone in 21 Days with Michael Mosley, in a bid to myth-bust some of the common misconceptions around healthy weight management. Using cutting-edge tests and technology to illustrate that the calories our volunteers actually need day to day, could be different to the NHS's recommended daily amounts, the show investigates whether weight loss inevitably leads to putting weight back on again, and explores whether exercise is as helpful as we think. There is also bespoke advice to each volunteer on how to achieve a healthy weight for the long term

programme.title: Star Trek Beyond (2016)
programme.desc: The USS Enterprise sets out on a rescue mission which turns out to be a deadly trap set by a mysterious warlord. The crew are left stranded on an unexplored planet, and join forces with a fugitive alien scavenger to find a way off the hostile world and prevent a terrible weapon being unleashed upon the galaxy. Sci-fi adventure, starring Chris Pine, Zachary Quinto and Idris Elba

programme.title: Star Trek Into Darkness (2013)
programme.desc: Captain Kirk and the crew of the starship Enterprise are sent on a clandestine mission into enemy territory to track down the rogue Starfleet agent behind a terrorist attack on Earth. However, the assignment brings to light a hidden threat from within their own organisation. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Chris Pine, Zachary Quinto and Benedict Cumberbatch

programme.title: The Sex Clinic
programme.desc: Experts cut through taboos about sexual health and help people get their sex lives back on track. Marco's discovered a lump on his penis. Harriette has her dry vagina checked over

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson hosts the dating show. This time, 30-year-old Stephanie, a self-confessed 'dating disaster', and 28-year-old Bethany, a free-spirited artist from Essex, choose a date from a selection of six naked people

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer search across two countries for two homes. Kirstie is with serial renters Mike and Poppy. Mike has lived in 18 homes, but Poppy wants to put down roots and they've chosen Cheshire as the right spot for their family. However, living in a high spec rental has given them great expectations for the home they'd love to own. Phil is in north Wales, helping nature-loving Pip, who's in search of a rural retreat, a chocolate-box character cottage with a garden big enough for chickens
